**[Lock-Up Agreements]{.underline}**

Each of our directors, executive officers, and holders of more than 1% of our outstanding common stock have entered into lock-up agreements with the underwriters providing that, subject to certain exceptions, they will not, without the prior written consent of Hargrove Securities LLC, sell, contract to sell, pledge, grant any option to purchase, make any short sale, or otherwise dispose of any shares of our common stock or any securities convertible into or exchangeable for shares of our common stock for a period of 180 days following the date of this prospectus.

The lock-up agreements contain customary exceptions, including transfers of shares: (a) as bona fide gifts to family members or trusts for the benefit of the locked-up party or immediate family members; (b) by will or intestacy; (c) pursuant to a qualified domestic relations order; (d) to affiliates or limited partners, members, or stockholders of the locked-up party; (e) as part of pro rata distributions to partners, members, or stockholders of the locked-up party; and (f) pursuant to trading plans established under Rule 10b5-1 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, provided that any such plan was in effect prior to the date of the lock-up agreement and any required public filings clearly note the existence of the lock-up agreement; in each case, provided that the transferee agrees in writing to be bound by the terms of the lock-up agreement.

Hargrove Securities LLC may, in its sole discretion, release any of the securities subject to the lock-up agreements at any time, subject to providing three business days\' prior notice to the Company for any release covering a material number of shares.

**[IX. Material Contracts]{.underline}**

*The following is a summary of certain material contracts to which we are a party. These summaries do not purport to be complete and are subject to, and qualified in their entirety by reference to, the full text of the applicable agreements, which are filed as exhibits to the registration statement of which this prospectus forms a part.*

**[IX.A. Revolving Credit Facility --- Oakvale National Bank]{.underline}**

On July 15, 2022, we entered into a credit agreement with Oakvale National Bank, a national banking association, providing for a revolving credit facility in an aggregate principal amount of up to \$40,000,000. As of December 31, 2024, \$22,500,000 was outstanding under this facility. Borrowings under the facility bear interest at a rate per annum equal to the Secured Overnight Financing Rate (SOFR) plus 2.50%. The facility matures on December 31, 2026.

**Mandatory Prepayment Provisions.** The credit agreement requires mandatory prepayment of 50% of the net cash proceeds received by the Company from any equity issuance (including this offering) to the extent that such net proceeds exceed \$150,000,000. Net cash proceeds are defined in the credit agreement as gross proceeds less underwriting discounts and commissions and reasonable and documented out-of-pocket offering expenses. This mandatory prepayment is required to be made within five business days following the receipt of the applicable net cash proceeds.

**Financial Covenants.** The credit agreement requires us to maintain (i) minimum unrestricted cash and cash equivalents of \$15,000,000 at all times, (ii) a maximum total leverage ratio (as defined in the credit agreement) of not more than 3.00 to 1.00, tested quarterly, and (iii) a minimum interest coverage ratio (as defined in the credit agreement) of not less than 3.00 to 1.00, tested quarterly.

**Negative Covenants.** The credit agreement contains customary negative covenants that, among other things, restrict our ability to: (a) incur additional indebtedness, (b) create or permit liens on our assets, (c) make investments, loans, or advances, (d) pay dividends or make other restricted payments, (e) sell or otherwise dispose of assets, (f) enter into transactions with affiliates, and (g) engage in mergers, consolidations, or fundamental changes.

**Change of Control.** The credit agreement provides that a change of control constitutes an event of default, triggering acceleration and mandatory repayment of all outstanding borrowings. A change of control is defined to include the acquisition by any person or group (as such terms are used in Sections 13(d) and 14(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) of direct or indirect beneficial ownership of more than 50% of our outstanding voting stock. The completion of this offering does not constitute a change of control under the credit agreement.

**Events of Default.** The credit agreement contains customary events of default, including failure to make payments when due, breach of representations or covenants, cross-default to other material indebtedness, bankruptcy and insolvency events, material adverse change, and the occurrence of a change of control.

**Collateral.** Our obligations under the credit agreement are secured by a first-priority security interest in substantially all of our assets, including accounts receivable, inventory, equipment, intellectual property, and general intangibles.

**[IX.B. Exclusive License Agreement --- University of Texas at Austin]{.underline}**

On July 1, 2018, we entered into an exclusive license agreement with the Board of Regents of the University of Texas System, on behalf of the University of Texas at Austin, for certain bioelectric signal processing technology. The license agreement has a remaining term through June 30, 2033.

**Scope.** The license agreement grants us an exclusive, worldwide license under certain patented and patent-pending bioelectric signal processing algorithms and related know-how for the development, manufacture, and commercialization of wearable cardiac monitoring devices.

**Royalty.** We are required to pay a royalty of 3.5% of net sales of products incorporating the licensed technology, payable quarterly in arrears within 45 days after the end of each calendar quarter. We are also required to pay a minimum annual royalty of \$500,000, creditable against earned royalties.

**Diligence Obligations.** We are required to use commercially reasonable efforts to develop and commercialize products incorporating the licensed technology, including the achievement of certain development and commercial milestones specified in the agreement.

**Sublicensing.** We may grant sublicenses under the licensed technology with the prior written consent of the University, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed.

**Termination.** The University may terminate the license agreement upon material breach by us, subject to a 90-day cure period following written notice of such breach, or if we cease active development efforts for a continuous period of 12 months or more. We may terminate the license agreement at any time upon 180 days\' prior written notice and payment of all accrued and unpaid royalties.

**Assignment.** The license agreement provides that it may not be assigned by either party without the prior written consent of the other party, except that the Company may assign the agreement in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of substantially all of its assets.

**Representations.** The University represents that it is the owner of the licensed technology and has the right to grant the licenses contemplated by the agreement.

**[IX.C. Supply Agreement --- Tanaka Precision Components Ltd.]{.underline}**

On March 15, 2021, we entered into a supply agreement with Tanaka Precision Components Ltd., a Japanese corporation, for the supply of proprietary biosensor arrays used in our PulseGuard Pro cardiac rhythm monitoring devices. The agreement has a remaining term through December 31, 2027.

**Minimum Purchase Commitment.** We have a minimum annual purchase commitment of \$12,000,000 under the supply agreement.

**Exclusivity.** Tanaka has agreed to supply the biosensor arrays exclusively to us within the cardiac rhythm monitoring field for the term of the agreement.

**Pricing.** The supply agreement provides for fixed pricing, subject to annual adjustment based on changes in the Japanese Consumer Price Index. Pricing is subject to renegotiation in good faith in the event of material changes in the cost of raw materials used in the manufacture of the biosensor arrays.

**Quality Standards.** The biosensor arrays must conform to specifications set forth in the agreement and are subject to our inspection and quality assurance testing upon delivery. Tanaka provides a warranty that all products delivered will conform to the applicable specifications and will be free from defects in materials and workmanship for a period of 24 months from delivery.

**Termination.** Either party may terminate the supply agreement upon material breach by the other party, subject to a 60-day cure period following written notice. Tanaka may also terminate the agreement if we fail to meet the minimum annual purchase commitment for two consecutive calendar years.

**Force Majeure.** The supply agreement contains customary force majeure provisions excusing performance (other than payment obligations) in the event of circumstances beyond the reasonable control of the affected party, including natural disasters, acts of war, government action, and epidemics.

**Governing Law.** The supply agreement is governed by the laws of Japan, and any disputes arising thereunder are subject to binding arbitration in Tokyo, Japan under the rules of the Japan Commercial Arbitration Association.

**[IX.D. 2024 Equity Incentive Plan]{.underline}**

On November 15, 2024, our board of directors adopted the 2024 Equity Incentive Plan, which was subsequently approved by our stockholders on December 20, 2024. The 2024 Equity Incentive Plan provides for the grant of stock options (both incentive stock options and nonstatutory stock options), restricted stock, restricted stock units, stock appreciation rights, and other stock-based awards to our employees, directors, and consultants.

**Share Reserve.** A total of 6,000,000 shares of common stock have been authorized for issuance under the plan. As of December 31, 2024, 4,200,000 shares were subject to outstanding stock options at a weighted-average exercise price of \$6.50 per share, and 1,800,000 shares remained available for future grants.

**Administration.** The plan is administered by the Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors, which has the authority to determine the participants, types of awards, number of shares, exercise prices, vesting schedules, and other terms and conditions of awards granted under the plan.

**Key Terms.** Stock options granted under the plan have a maximum term of 10 years and an exercise price of not less than 100% of the fair market value of a share of common stock on the date of grant. Awards generally vest over a four-year period from the date of grant, subject to continued service.

**Change of Control Provisions.** The plan provides for accelerated vesting of outstanding awards upon a qualifying termination of employment (termination without cause or resignation for good reason) occurring within 12 months following a change of control of the Company.

**[X. Legal Proceedings]{.underline}**

**[X.A. VitaBand Corp. Patent Infringement Litigation]{.underline}**

On June 14, 2024, VitaBand Corp. filed a complaint against the Company in the United States District Court for the Eastern District of Texas (Case No. 6:24-cv-00891), alleging that the Company\'s PulseGuard Pro cardiac rhythm monitoring device infringes U.S. Patent No. 10,456,223, which is owned by VitaBand Corp. The complaint alleges both literal infringement and infringement under the doctrine of equivalents. VitaBand Corp. seeks monetary damages in the amount of \$45,000,000, plus enhanced damages for alleged willful infringement, attorneys\' fees, costs, and permanent injunctive relief that would prohibit the Company from making, using, selling, offering to sell, or importing the PulseGuard Pro device in the United States.

On August 12, 2024, the Company filed its answer denying VitaBand Corp.\'s allegations and asserting affirmative defenses, including non-infringement, invalidity, and unenforceability of U.S. Patent No. 10,456,223. The Company also filed a counterclaim seeking a declaratory judgment of non-infringement and invalidity of the asserted patent. Fact discovery is ongoing. A Markman hearing has been scheduled for June 2025.

The Company believes the claims asserted by VitaBand Corp. are without merit and intends to vigorously defend against this action. However, the outcome of litigation is inherently uncertain, and there can be no assurance that the Company will prevail. No loss reserve has been established with respect to this matter because the Company has determined that a loss is not probable at this time.

**[X.B. EEOC Charge of Discrimination]{.underline}**

On September 3, 2024, a former employee of the Company filed a charge of discrimination with the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission alleging age discrimination in violation of the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967, as amended, in connection with the termination of the employee\'s employment in July 2024. The Company filed its position statement with the EEOC on November 1, 2024, in which it denied the allegations and provided supporting documentation.

The Company\'s potential exposure in connection with this charge is estimated at \$250,000 to \$500,000. The Company has established a reserve of \$350,000 with respect to this matter as of December 31, 2024.

**[X.C. General]{.underline}**

From time to time, the Company is involved in various claims, lawsuits, and proceedings arising in the ordinary course of business. Other than as described above, the Company is not currently party to any material pending legal proceedings that, in the opinion of management, are likely to have a material adverse effect on the Company\'s business, financial condition, or results of operations.

**[XI. Underwriting]{.underline}**

**[XI.A. Underwriters and Syndicate]{.underline}**

Hargrove Securities LLC is acting as the lead book-running manager of this offering, and Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc. is acting as co-manager. Subject to the terms and conditions set forth in an underwriting agreement among us, the Selling Stockholders, and the underwriters, we and the Selling Stockholders have agreed to sell to the underwriters, and each of the underwriters has severally agreed to purchase from us and the Selling Stockholders, the number of shares of common stock set forth opposite such underwriter\'s name below:

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Underwriter**                     **Number of Shares**
  ----------------------------------- -----------------------------------
  Hargrove Securities LLC             8,400,000

  Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc.   3,600,000

  **Total**                           **12,000,000**
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------

Subject to the terms and conditions of the underwriting agreement, each underwriter has severally agreed to purchase, and the Company and the Selling Stockholders have agreed to sell to such underwriter, the number of shares of common stock set forth opposite such underwriter\'s name above. The underwriting agreement provides that the obligations of the underwriters to purchase the shares included in this offering are subject to certain conditions precedent, including the absence of any material adverse change in our business, the receipt of certain legal opinions, officer\'s certificates, and comfort letters, and other customary closing conditions. The underwriters are committed to purchase all of the shares offered by us and the Selling Stockholders if they purchase any such shares.

**[XI.B. Over-Allotment Option]{.underline}**

We have granted to the underwriters an option, exercisable for 30 days from the closing date of this offering, to purchase up to 1,800,000 additional shares of common stock at the initial public offering price per share, less the underwriting discount, solely to cover over-allotments, if any. If the underwriters exercise this option, each underwriter will be obligated, subject to conditions contained in the underwriting agreement, to purchase a number of additional shares approximately proportionate to that underwriter\'s initial allocation set forth in the table above. If the underwriters\' option is exercised in full, the total public offering price, underwriting discount, and proceeds (before expenses) to us and to the Selling Stockholders will be as set forth in the table below.

**[XI.C. Underwriting Discount and Expenses]{.underline}**

The following table shows the per share and total public offering price, underwriting discount, and proceeds, before expenses, to us and the Selling Stockholders, assuming both no exercise and full exercise of the underwriters\' over-allotment option:

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
                                     **Per Share**     **Total Without Over-Allotment**   **Total With Over-Allotment**
  ---------------------------------- ----------------- ---------------------------------- -------------------------------
  Public offering price              \$**\_***\_*      \$**\_***\_*                       \$**\_***\_*

  Underwriting discount              \$**\_***\_*      \$**\_***\_*                       \$**\_***\_*

  Proceeds, before expenses, to us   \$**\_***\_*      \$**\_***\_*                       \$**\_***\_*

  Proceeds to Selling Stockholders   \$**\_***\_*      \$**\_***\_*                       \$**\_***\_*
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The underwriting discount is equal to the public offering price per share less the amount per share the underwriters pay to us and the Selling Stockholders. We have agreed to pay the underwriting discount set forth in the table above with respect to the shares sold by us and the Selling Stockholders in this offering.

We estimate that the total expenses of this offering, excluding the underwriting discount, will be approximately \$3,200,000, all of which are payable by us. These expenses include SEC registration fees, Nasdaq listing fees, Financial Industry Regulatory Authority filing fees, printing and engraving expenses, transfer agent fees, and the fees and expenses of our legal counsel, Stonebridge & Calloway LLP, and our auditors, Whitman Reese & Co. The underwriters are responsible for the fees and expenses of their own counsel, Ashford & Pine LLP, which are estimated at \$850,000.

**[XI.D. Lock-Up Agreements]{.underline}**

We and each of our directors, executive officers, and holders of more than 1% of our outstanding shares of common stock have agreed with the underwriters, subject to certain exceptions, not to dispose of or hedge any shares of our common stock or securities convertible into or exchangeable for shares of our common stock during the period from the date of this prospectus continuing through the date 180 days after the date of this prospectus (the \"lock-up period\"), without the prior written consent of Hargrove Securities LLC. Specifically, these agreements provide that, during the lock-up period, the locked-up parties will not, directly or indirectly: (a) offer, pledge, sell, contract to sell, grant any option, right, or warrant to purchase, or otherwise transfer or dispose of any shares of common stock or any securities convertible into or exercisable or exchangeable for common stock; (b) enter into any swap or other arrangement that transfers, in whole or in part, any of the economic consequences of ownership of common stock; or (c) make any demand for, or exercise any right with respect to, the registration of any shares of common stock or any securities convertible into or exercisable or exchangeable for common stock.

The lock-up agreements contain customary exceptions, including transfers (i) as bona fide gifts or for bona fide estate planning purposes, (ii) by will or intestacy, (iii) pursuant to a qualified domestic relations order, (iv) to affiliates, limited partners, members, or stockholders of the locked-up party, (v) as part of pro rata distributions, and (vi) pursuant to pre-existing trading plans under Rule 10b5-1 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, in each case provided that the transferee agrees in writing to be bound by the terms of the lock-up agreement.

Hargrove Securities LLC may, in its sole discretion, release all or any portion of the securities subject to the lock-up agreements at any time.

**[XI.E. Indemnification]{.underline}**

We and the Selling Stockholders have agreed to indemnify the underwriters against certain liabilities, including liabilities under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and to contribute to payments that the underwriters may be required to make in respect of such liabilities.

**[XI.F. Nasdaq Listing]{.underline}**

We have applied to list our shares of common stock on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol \"MPLS.\"

**[XI.G. Stabilization, Short Positions, and Penalty Bids]{.underline}**

In connection with this offering, the underwriters may engage in stabilizing transactions, short sales, and purchases to cover positions created by short sales, and penalty bids, in accordance with Regulation M under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended.

> • *Stabilizing transactions* permit bids to purchase shares so long as the stabilizing bids do not exceed a specified maximum and are engaged in for the purpose of preventing or retarding a decline in the market price of the shares while the offering is in progress.
>
> • *Short sales* involve the sale by the underwriters of a greater number of shares than they are required to purchase in the offering. \"Covered\" short sales are sales made in an amount not greater than the underwriters\' option to purchase additional shares in this offering. The underwriters may close out any covered short position by either exercising their over-allotment option or purchasing shares in the open market. \"Naked\" short sales are sales in excess of the over-allotment option. The underwriters must close out any naked short position by purchasing shares in the open market.
>
> • *Penalty bids* permit the underwriters to reclaim a selling concession from a dealer when the shares originally sold by the dealer are purchased in a stabilizing or syndicate covering transaction to cover short positions.

These activities by the underwriters, as well as other purchases by the underwriters for their own accounts, may stabilize, maintain, or otherwise affect the market price of the shares. As a result, the price of the shares may be higher than the price that otherwise might exist in the open market. These transactions may be effected on the Nasdaq Global Select Market, in the over-the-counter market, or otherwise, and if commenced, may be discontinued at any time.

Neither we nor any of the underwriters make any representation or prediction as to the direction or magnitude of any effect that the transactions described above may have on the price of the shares.

**[XI.H. Electronic Distribution]{.underline}**

A prospectus in electronic format may be made available on websites maintained by one or more of the underwriters or selling group members, if any, participating in this offering. The underwriters may agree to allocate a number of shares to selling group members for sale to their online brokerage account holders. Internet distributions will be allocated by the lead manager to selling group members that may make Internet distributions on the same basis as other allocations.

**[XII. Experts and Legal Matters]{.underline}**

**[Legal Matters]{.underline}**

The validity of the shares of common stock offered hereby will be passed upon for us by Stonebridge & Calloway LLP, Austin, Texas. Certain legal matters in connection with this offering will be passed upon for the underwriters by Ashford & Pine LLP, New York, New York.

**[Experts]{.underline}**

The financial statements of Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc. as of and for the year ended December 31, 2024, included in this prospectus, have been audited by Whitman Reese & Co., independent registered public accounting firm, as stated in their report appearing herein, and are included in reliance upon such report given on the authority of such firm as experts in accounting and auditing. The principal office of Whitman Reese & Co. is located at 200 S. Wacker Drive, Suite 1500, Chicago, IL 60606.

**[Where You Can Find More Information]{.underline}**

We have filed with the SEC a registration statement on Form S-1 (File No. 333-284517) under the Securities Act with respect to the shares of common stock offered by this prospectus. This prospectus, which constitutes a part of the registration statement, does not contain all of the information set forth in the registration statement. For further information about us and the shares offered by this prospectus, reference is made to the registration statement and the exhibits and schedules filed as a part of the registration statement. Statements contained in this prospectus concerning the contents of any contract or any other document are not necessarily complete, and, in each instance, we refer you to the copy of the contract or other document filed as an exhibit to the registration statement. Each of these statements is qualified in all respects by this reference.

You may read and copy any materials we file with the SEC at the SEC\'s Public Reference Room at 100 F Street, N.E., Washington, D.C. 20549. You may obtain information on the operation of the Public Reference Room by calling the SEC at 1-800-SEC-0330. The SEC also maintains a website at www.sec.gov that contains reports, proxy and information statements, and other information regarding issuers, including Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc., that file electronically with the SEC.

Upon completion of this offering, we will be subject to the information and reporting requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and, in accordance with those requirements, will file periodic reports, proxy statements, and other information with the SEC. These periodic reports, proxy statements, and other information will be available for inspection and copying at the SEC\'s public reference facilities and the SEC\'s website referred to above.

**FOURTH:** The text of the Certificate of Incorporation of the Corporation is hereby amended and restated to read in its entirety as set forth below, effective as of December 20, 2024:

**[ARTICLE I --- NAME]{.underline}**

The name of the corporation is **Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc.** (the \"Corporation\").

**[ARTICLE II --- REGISTERED OFFICE AND REGISTERED AGENT]{.underline}**

The address of the registered office of the Corporation in the State of Delaware is Delaware Agent Center, 1301 Market Street, Wilmington, New Castle County, Delaware 19801. The name of the registered agent of the Corporation at such address is The Delaware Trust Company.

**[ARTICLE III --- PURPOSE]{.underline}**

The purpose of the Corporation is to engage in any lawful act or activity for which corporations may be organized under the DGCL. The Corporation shall have all powers necessary or convenient to carry out such purpose, as granted by the DGCL.

**[ARTICLE IV --- CAPITAL STOCK]{.underline}**

**Section 4.1 --- Authorized Shares**

The total number of shares of all classes of capital stock that the Corporation is authorized to issue is **210,000,000 shares**, consisting of (a) **200,000,000 shares of Common Stock**, par value **\$0.001 per share** (\"Common Stock\"), and (b) **10,000,000 shares of Preferred Stock**, par value **\$0.001 per share** (\"Preferred Stock\").

The number of authorized shares of Common Stock or Preferred Stock may be increased or decreased (but not below the number of shares thereof then outstanding) by the affirmative vote of the holders of a majority of the voting power of all of the then-outstanding shares of capital stock of the Corporation entitled to vote thereon, irrespective of the provisions of Section 242(b)(2) of the DGCL (or any successor provision thereto), and no vote of the holders of any of the Common Stock or the Preferred Stock voting separately as a class shall be required therefor.

**Section 4.2 --- Common Stock**

**Section 4.2.1 --- Voting Rights.** Each holder of Common Stock shall be entitled to one (1) vote for each share of Common Stock held of record by such holder on all matters submitted to a vote of the stockholders. The holders of Common Stock shall vote together as a single class on all matters submitted to a vote of the stockholders, except as otherwise required by law or provided herein.

Except as otherwise required by law, holders of Common Stock shall not be entitled to vote on any amendment to this Certificate of Incorporation (including any certificate of designation relating to any series of Preferred Stock) that relates solely to the terms of one or more outstanding series of Preferred Stock if the holders of such affected series are entitled, either separately or together with the holders of one or more other such series, to vote thereon pursuant to this Certificate of Incorporation or pursuant to the DGCL.

There shall be no cumulative voting in the election of directors or with respect to any other matter submitted to a vote of the stockholders.

**Section 4.2.2 --- Dividends.** Subject to the preferential rights of holders of any then-outstanding shares of Preferred Stock, the holders of Common Stock shall be entitled to receive ratably such dividends, if any, as may be declared from time to time by the Board of Directors of the Corporation out of funds legally available therefor.

**Section 4.2.3 --- Liquidation.** In the event of any voluntary or involuntary liquidation, dissolution, or winding up of the Corporation, after payment or provision for payment of the debts and other liabilities of the Corporation and the preferential amounts to which the holders of any then-outstanding shares of Preferred Stock are entitled, the holders of Common Stock shall be entitled to share ratably in the remaining assets of the Corporation available for distribution to its stockholders.

**Section 4.2.4 --- No Preemptive Rights.** No holder of Common Stock shall have any preemptive, subscription, or other right to purchase or subscribe for any shares of any class of capital stock of the Corporation, whether now or hereafter authorized, or any securities convertible into or exchangeable for any such shares.

**Section 4.3 --- Preferred Stock**

The Board of Directors is hereby expressly authorized, subject to any limitations prescribed by the DGCL, by resolution or resolutions, to provide from time to time for the issuance of shares of Preferred Stock in one or more series, without further approval of the stockholders, and to fix from time to time before issuance the number of shares to be included in each such series, the designation of each such series, and the powers, preferences, and relative, participating, optional, and other special rights of the shares of each such series, and the qualifications, limitations, and restrictions thereof, including but not limited to:

> \(a\) the distinctive serial designation of such series and the number of shares constituting such series, which number may be increased (except where otherwise provided by the Board of Directors in creating such series) or decreased (but not below the number of shares thereof then outstanding) from time to time by resolution of the Board of Directors;
>
> \(b\) the dividend rate (or the manner of determining such rate) applicable to shares of such series, whether dividends shall be cumulative and, if so, from what date or dates, and the conditions upon which and the dates when dividends shall be payable, and the relative rights of priority, if any, of payment of dividends on shares of such series;
>
> \(c\) whether shares of such series shall be redeemable, the conditions and dates upon which such shares shall be redeemable, and the redemption price or prices (which may vary under different conditions and at different redemption dates), including the form of consideration payable upon redemption;
>
> \(d\) the rights of the holders of shares of such series upon the voluntary or involuntary liquidation, dissolution, or winding up of the Corporation, and the relative rights of priority, if any, of payment with respect to shares of such series;
>
> \(e\) the terms and amount of any sinking fund provision for the redemption or purchase of shares of such series;
>
> \(f\) the terms and conditions, if any, upon which shares of such series may be converted into or exchanged for shares of any other class or classes of capital stock of the Corporation or any other series of Preferred Stock, including the price or prices or the rate or rates of conversion or exchange and the terms of adjustment, if any;
>
> \(g\) the voting rights, if any, of the holders of shares of such series, in addition to any voting rights required by the DGCL; and
>
> \(h\) any other powers, preferences, rights, qualifications, limitations, and restrictions as the Board of Directors may deem advisable and as are not inconsistent with the DGCL or this Certificate of Incorporation.

All shares of Preferred Stock of any one series shall be identical in all respects with all other shares of such series, except that shares of any one series issued at different times may differ as to the dates from which dividends thereon shall be cumulative.

As of the effective date of this Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation, all previously designated series of Preferred Stock (including any Series A Preferred Stock and Series B Preferred Stock previously outstanding) have been converted to Common Stock in accordance with their respective terms, and no shares of Preferred Stock are outstanding. All certificates of designation, preferences, and rights relating to such previously designated series are hereby superseded in their entirety. The authority to issue shares of Preferred Stock without further stockholder approval could be used by the Board of Directors for various corporate purposes, including the implementation of a stockholder rights plan.

**Section 4.4 --- Rights and Options**

The Corporation shall have the authority to create and issue rights, warrants, and options entitling the holders thereof to acquire from the Corporation any shares of its capital stock of any class or classes, with such rights, warrants, and options to be evidenced by or in such instrument or instruments as shall be approved by the Board of Directors. The Board of Directors is empowered to set the exercise price, the duration, the times and conditions for exercise, and all other terms and conditions of such rights, warrants, or options, including the consideration, if any, payable in connection with the issuance thereof. The terms of any such rights, warrants, or options may provide that the exercise price and the number of shares subject thereto shall be adjusted upon the occurrence of specified events, all as determined by the Board of Directors. This authority supports the Corporation\'s equity compensation plans, including any equity incentive plan adopted by the Board of Directors and approved by the stockholders of the Corporation.

**[ARTICLE V --- BOARD OF DIRECTORS]{.underline}**

**Section 5.1 --- Powers**

The business and affairs of the Corporation shall be managed by or under the direction of the Board of Directors. In addition to the powers and authority expressly conferred upon the Board of Directors by statute, this Certificate of Incorporation, or the Bylaws of the Corporation, the Board of Directors is hereby empowered to exercise all such powers and do all such acts and things as may be exercised or done by the Corporation. In furtherance and not in limitation of the powers conferred upon it by the DGCL, the Board of Directors is expressly authorized to make, adopt, amend, alter, or repeal the Bylaws of the Corporation.

**Section 5.2 --- Number of Directors**

The number of directors constituting the entire Board of Directors shall be fixed from time to time exclusively by resolution adopted by the Board of Directors, subject to any rights of the holders of any series of Preferred Stock then outstanding to elect additional directors under specified circumstances. The initial Board of Directors following the effective date of this Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation shall consist of seven (7) directors. No decrease in the authorized number of directors constituting the Board of Directors shall shorten the term of any incumbent director.

**Section 5.3 --- Classified Board Structure**

The Board of Directors shall be divided into three classes, designated as Class I, Class II, and Class III, as nearly equal in number as reasonably possible. The Board of Directors is authorized to assign members of the Board of Directors already in office to the initial classes. The initial assignment of directors to classes shall be made by resolution of the Board of Directors.

> \(a\) **Class I Directors.** The initial term of office of Class I Directors shall expire at the first annual meeting of stockholders to be held following the effective date of this Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation, which meeting is expected to be held in calendar year 2026. At such meeting, successors to the Class I Directors shall be elected for a full term expiring at the annual meeting of stockholders held in 2029.
>
> \(b\) **Class II Directors.** The initial term of office of Class II Directors shall expire at the annual meeting of stockholders expected to be held in calendar year 2027. At such meeting, successors to the Class II Directors shall be elected for a full term expiring at the annual meeting of stockholders held in 2030.
>
> \(c\) **Class III Directors.** The initial term of office of Class III Directors shall expire at the annual meeting of stockholders expected to be held in calendar year 2028. At such meeting, successors to the Class III Directors shall be elected for a full term expiring at the annual meeting of stockholders held in 2031.

Commencing with the first annual meeting of stockholders following the effective date of this Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation, directors elected to succeed those directors whose terms shall then have expired shall be elected for a term of office expiring at the third succeeding annual meeting of stockholders after their election. Each director shall hold office until such director\'s successor shall have been duly elected and qualified, or until such director\'s earlier death, resignation, disqualification, or removal.

**Section 5.4 --- Election of Directors**

Election of directors need not be by written ballot unless the Bylaws of the Corporation shall so provide. Directors shall be elected by a plurality of the votes cast at any meeting of stockholders at which a quorum is present and at which directors are to be elected.

**Section 5.5 --- Removal**

Subject to the rights of the holders of any series of Preferred Stock then outstanding, any director or the entire Board of Directors may be removed from office at any time, but only for cause and only by the affirmative vote of the holders of at least sixty-six and two-thirds percent (66⅔%) of the voting power of all then-outstanding shares of capital stock of the Corporation entitled to vote generally in the election of directors, voting together as a single class.

**Section 5.6 --- Vacancies**

Subject to the rights of the holders of any series of Preferred Stock then outstanding, any vacancy on the Board of Directors, however occurring, including a vacancy resulting from an enlargement of the Board of Directors, shall be filled only by vote of a majority of the directors then in office, even though less than a quorum, or by a sole remaining director, and shall not be filled by the stockholders. Any director elected to fill a vacancy shall hold office for the remainder of the full term of the class of directors in which the vacancy occurred and until such director\'s successor shall have been duly elected and qualified, or until such director\'s earlier death, resignation, disqualification, or removal. No decrease in the authorized number of directors shall shorten the term of any incumbent director.

**[ARTICLE VI --- STOCKHOLDER MATTERS]{.underline}**

**Section 6.1 --- No Action by Written Consent**

Subject to the rights of the holders of any series of Preferred Stock then outstanding, any action required or permitted to be taken by the stockholders of the Corporation must be effected at a duly called annual or special meeting of the stockholders of the Corporation and may not be effected by any consent in writing by such stockholders in lieu of such meeting.

**Section 6.2 --- Special Meetings**

Special meetings of the stockholders of the Corporation may be called only by the Board of Directors pursuant to a resolution adopted by a majority of the Whole Board. For purposes of this Certificate of Incorporation, the term \"Whole Board\" shall mean the total number of authorized directorships, whether or not there exist any vacancies or other unfilled seats on the Board of Directors. Special meetings of the stockholders may not be called by any other person or persons, including the stockholders. Business transacted at any special meeting of stockholders shall be limited to the purposes stated in the notice of such meeting.

**Section 6.3 --- Advance Notice**

Advance notice of stockholder nominations for the election of directors and other business to be brought before any meeting of the stockholders of the Corporation shall be given in the manner and to the extent provided in the Bylaws of the Corporation.

**[ARTICLE VII --- LIMITATION OF DIRECTOR LIABILITY; EXCULPATION]{.underline}**

**Section 7.1 --- Limitation of Personal Liability**

To the fullest extent permitted by the DGCL, as the same exists or as may hereafter be amended (but, in the case of any such amendment, only to the extent that such amendment permits the Corporation to provide broader exculpation than permitted prior thereto), no director or officer of the Corporation shall be personally liable to the Corporation or its stockholders for monetary damages for breach of fiduciary duty as a director or officer. Without limiting the foregoing, and for purposes of clarity in accordance with Section 102(b)(7) of the DGCL, as amended effective August 1, 2022, this provision shall eliminate and limit the personal liability of both directors and officers to the fullest extent permitted by the DGCL.

Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Article VII shall not eliminate or limit the liability of a director or officer:

> \(i\) for any breach of the director\'s or officer\'s duty of loyalty to the Corporation or its stockholders;
>
> \(ii\) for acts or omissions not in good faith or which involve intentional misconduct or a knowing violation of law;
>
> \(iii\) for directors, under Section 174 of the DGCL (relating to the unlawful payment of dividends or unlawful stock purchases or redemptions);
>
> \(iv\) for any transaction from which the director or officer derived an improper personal benefit; or
>
> \(v\) for officers, in any action by or in the right of the Corporation (i.e., a derivative action).

If the DGCL is amended after the effective date of this Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation to authorize corporate action further eliminating or limiting the personal liability of directors or officers, then the liability of a director or officer of the Corporation shall be eliminated or limited to the fullest extent permitted by the DGCL, as so amended.

Any repeal or modification of this Section 7.1 by the stockholders of the Corporation shall not adversely affect any right or protection of any director or officer of the Corporation existing at the time of, or increase the liability of any director or officer with respect to any acts or omissions occurring prior to, such repeal or modification.

**Section 7.2 --- Indemnification**

The Corporation shall indemnify and hold harmless, to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law as it presently exists or may hereafter be amended, any person (a \"Covered Person\") who was or is made or is threatened to be made a party or is otherwise involved in any action, suit, or proceeding, whether civil, criminal, administrative, or investigative (a \"Proceeding\"), by reason of the fact that he or she, or a person for whom he or she is the legal representative, is or was a director or officer of the Corporation or, while serving as a director or officer of the Corporation, is or was serving at the request of the Corporation as a director, officer, employee, or agent of another corporation, partnership, joint venture, trust, enterprise, or nonprofit entity, including service with respect to an employee benefit plan, against all liability and loss suffered (including judgments, fines, ERISA excise taxes or penalties, and amounts paid in settlement) and expenses (including attorneys\' fees and disbursements) reasonably incurred by such Covered Person in connection with such Proceeding.

Notwithstanding the preceding sentence, except as otherwise provided in the Bylaws of the Corporation, the Corporation shall be required to indemnify a Covered Person in connection with a Proceeding (or part thereof) commenced by such Covered Person only if the commencement of such Proceeding (or part thereof) was authorized by the Board of Directors.

The Corporation shall, to the fullest extent not prohibited by applicable law, pay the expenses (including attorneys\' fees and disbursements) incurred by a Covered Person in defending any Proceeding in advance of its final disposition; provided, however, that, to the extent required by applicable law, the payment of expenses incurred by a Covered Person in advance of the final disposition of such Proceeding shall be made only upon receipt of an undertaking by or on behalf of such Covered Person to repay all amounts advanced if it shall ultimately be determined by final judicial decision from which there is no further right to appeal that such Covered Person is not entitled to be indemnified under this Section 7.2 or otherwise.

The Corporation may, to the extent authorized from time to time by the Board of Directors, grant rights to indemnification and to the advancement of expenses to any employee or agent of the Corporation to the fullest extent of the provisions of this Section 7.2 with respect to the indemnification and advancement of expenses of directors and officers of the Corporation.

The rights to indemnification and advancement of expenses conferred by this Section 7.2 shall be contract rights that vest at the time of such person\'s service to or at the request of the Corporation and shall continue as to a Covered Person who has ceased to be a director or officer and shall inure to the benefit of such person\'s heirs, executors, and administrators.

The rights conferred on any Covered Person by this Section 7.2 shall not be exclusive of any other rights which any such Covered Person may have or hereafter acquire under any statute, provision of this Certificate of Incorporation, the Bylaws, any agreement, any vote of stockholders or disinterested directors, or otherwise.

The Corporation may maintain insurance, at its expense, to protect itself and any director, officer, employee, or agent of the Corporation or of any other corporation, partnership, joint venture, trust, enterprise, or nonprofit entity against any expense, liability, or loss, whether or not the Corporation would have the power to indemnify such person against such expense, liability, or loss under the DGCL.

**[ARTICLE VIII --- AMENDMENT OF CERTIFICATE OF INCORPORATION]{.underline}**

The Corporation reserves the right to amend or repeal any provision contained in this Certificate of Incorporation, in the manner now or hereafter prescribed by the DGCL, and all rights and powers conferred upon stockholders, directors, and officers herein are granted subject to this reservation.

Notwithstanding anything contained in this Certificate of Incorporation to the contrary, and notwithstanding the fact that a lesser percentage or separate class vote may be specified by law, this Certificate of Incorporation, or the Bylaws, the affirmative vote of the holders of at least sixty-six and two-thirds percent (66⅔%) of the voting power of all then-outstanding shares of capital stock of the Corporation entitled to vote generally in the election of directors, voting together as a single class, shall be required to amend or repeal, or to adopt any provision inconsistent with, Article V (Board of Directors), Article VI (Stockholder Matters), Article VII (Limitation of Director Liability; Exculpation), this Article VIII (Amendment of Certificate of Incorporation), or Article X (Exclusive Forum Provisions) of this Certificate of Incorporation.

**[ARTICLE IX --- AMENDMENT OF BYLAWS]{.underline}**

In furtherance and not in limitation of the powers conferred upon the Board of Directors by the DGCL and this Certificate of Incorporation, the Board of Directors is expressly authorized to adopt, amend, alter, or repeal the Bylaws of the Corporation by the affirmative vote of a majority of the Whole Board.

In addition to any vote of the Board of Directors required by law, this Certificate of Incorporation, or the Bylaws of the Corporation, the Bylaws may also be adopted, amended, altered, or repealed by the stockholders of the Corporation; provided, however, that in addition to any vote of the stockholders required by law or by this Certificate of Incorporation, the affirmative vote of the holders of at least sixty-six and two-thirds percent (66⅔%) of the voting power of all then-outstanding shares of capital stock of the Corporation entitled to vote generally in the election of directors, voting together as a single class, shall be required for the stockholders to adopt, amend, alter, or repeal any provision of the Bylaws of the Corporation.

**[ARTICLE X --- EXCLUSIVE FORUM PROVISIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 10.1 --- Delaware Forum for Internal Corporate Claims**

Unless the Corporation consents in writing to the selection of an alternative forum, the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware (or, if the Court of Chancery does not have subject matter jurisdiction thereof, any other state court located within the State of Delaware, or, if no state court located within the State of Delaware has subject matter jurisdiction thereof, the federal district court for the District of Delaware) shall, to the fullest extent permitted by law, be the sole and exclusive forum for each of the following types of actions or proceedings:

> \(a\) any derivative action or proceeding brought on behalf of the Corporation;
>
> \(b\) any action asserting a claim of breach of a fiduciary duty owed by any current or former director, officer, employee, or agent of the Corporation to the Corporation or the Corporation\'s stockholders;
>
> \(c\) any action asserting a claim arising pursuant to any provision of the DGCL, this Certificate of Incorporation, or the Bylaws of the Corporation (as each may be amended from time to time);
>
> \(d\) any action to interpret, apply, enforce, or determine the validity of this Certificate of Incorporation or the Bylaws of the Corporation (including any right, obligation, or remedy thereunder);
>
> \(e\) any action asserting a claim governed by the internal affairs doctrine; or
>
> \(f\) any action asserting an \"internal corporate claim\" as that term is defined in Section 115 of the DGCL.

Any person or entity purchasing or otherwise acquiring or holding any interest in shares of capital stock of the Corporation shall be deemed to have notice of and to have consented to the provisions of this Section 10.1.

**Section 10.2 --- Federal Forum for Securities Act Claims**

Unless the Corporation consents in writing to the selection of an alternative forum, the federal district courts of the United States of America shall, to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, be the sole and exclusive forum for the resolution of any complaint asserting a cause of action arising under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the \"Securities Act\").

This provision is adopted in accordance with the Delaware Supreme Court\'s decision in *Salzberg v. Sciabacucchi*, 227 A.3d 102 (Del. 2020), which upheld the facial validity of federal forum provisions for Securities Act claims in certificates of incorporation of Delaware corporations. This provision is intended to benefit and may be enforced by the Corporation, its officers and directors, any underwriter or other professional or entity retained by the Corporation in connection with any offering of the Corporation\'s securities, and any other professional or entity whose consent is required under the Securities Act.

For the avoidance of doubt, this Section 10.2 shall not apply to any action asserting a cause of action arising under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, for which the federal courts of the United States of America have exclusive jurisdiction as provided by statute.

Any person or entity purchasing or otherwise acquiring or holding any interest in shares of capital stock of the Corporation shall be deemed to have notice of and to have consented to the provisions of this Section 10.2.

**Section 10.3 --- Severability of Forum Provisions**

If any provision of this Article X or any application thereof to any person or circumstance shall be held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable in any respect by a court of competent jurisdiction, the validity, legality, and enforceability of the remaining provisions of this Article X in all other respects, and the application of such provision to other persons or circumstances, shall not in any way be affected or impaired thereby, and such provision shall be interpreted so as reasonably to effect the intent of the parties hereto.

**[ARTICLE XI --- CORPORATE OPPORTUNITIES]{.underline}**

To the fullest extent permitted by Section 122(17) of the DGCL, the Corporation hereby renounces any interest or expectancy of the Corporation in, or in being offered an opportunity to participate in, any business opportunity that is from time to time presented to any director or officer of the Corporation who is also a director, officer, employee, partner, or member of any entity that is a stockholder of the Corporation or an affiliate of such stockholder (each such person, a \"Specified Party\"), unless such business opportunity is expressly offered to such person solely in his or her capacity as a director or officer of the Corporation. No Specified Party shall have any duty to communicate or offer any such business opportunity to the Corporation, and no Specified Party shall be liable to the Corporation or its stockholders for breach of any fiduciary or other duty by reason of the fact that such Specified Party pursues or acquires such business opportunity, directs such business opportunity to another person or entity, or fails to present such business opportunity, or information regarding such business opportunity, to the Corporation.

Neither the alteration, amendment, addition to, or repeal of this Article XI, nor the adoption of any provision of this Certificate of Incorporation inconsistent with this Article XI, shall apply to or have any effect on the liability or alleged liability of any Specified Party for or with respect to any business opportunity of which such Specified Party becomes aware prior to such alteration, amendment, addition, repeal, or adoption.

**[ARTICLE XII --- SEVERABILITY]{.underline}**

If any provision of this Certificate of Incorporation becomes or is declared on any ground by a court of competent jurisdiction to be illegal, unenforceable, or void, portions of such provision that are not illegal, unenforceable, or void shall remain in full force and effect, and the illegal, unenforceable, or void portions shall be deemed amended to the minimum extent necessary to make such provision legal, enforceable, and valid. In the event that any such provision is held to be entirely invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, such provision shall be deemed deleted from this Certificate of Incorporation, but every other provision of this Certificate of Incorporation shall remain in full force and effect.

**[ARTICLE XIII --- MISCELLANEOUS]{.underline}**

**Section 13.1 --- Incorporator**

The original incorporator of the Corporation was Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy, whose mailing address at the time of original incorporation was 4200 Clearwater Blvd., Suite 800, Austin, Texas 78759. The powers of the incorporator terminated upon the filing of the original Certificate of Incorporation with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware on March 14, 2016.

**Section 13.2 --- Effective Date**

This Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation shall become effective upon its filing with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware.

*\[Remainder of page intentionally left blank; signature page follows.\]*

**IN WITNESS WHEREOF**, the undersigned has executed this Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation as of December 20, 2024.

**MERIDIAN PULSE TECHNOLOGIES, INC.**

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Title: General Counsel & Secretary

**[SUMMARY OF KEY TERMS --- REVOLVING CREDIT AGREEMENT]{.underline}**

**Prepared by Stonebridge & Calloway LLP for Internal Deal Team Use** **in Connection with the Initial Public Offering of Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc.**

**Date of Preparation:** March 2025

**NOTICE:** This summary is provided for reference purposes only and does not constitute a complete description of all terms and conditions of the Credit Agreement. Reference should be made to the Credit Agreement itself for the complete terms. This document constitutes attorney work product prepared in connection with IPO due diligence and is subject to attorney-client privilege. Distribution is limited to the internal deal team.

**[Section 1: Overview and Parties]{.underline}**

> •**Agreement:** Revolving Credit Agreement dated as of June 15, 2023 (as amended by that certain First Amendment to Revolving Credit Agreement dated as of February 28, 2024, the \"Credit Agreement\").
>
> •**Borrower:** Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc., a Delaware corporation, with principal offices at 4200 Clearwater Blvd., Suite 800, Austin, Texas 78759. Federal EIN: 82-4937215.
>
> •**Lender / Administrative Agent:** Ridgemont National Bank, a national banking association.
>
> •**Guarantors:** All existing and future domestic subsidiaries of the Borrower. Note: As of the date hereof, the Company has no material domestic subsidiaries. The guaranty provisions are therefore currently operative only with respect to future subsidiaries.
>
> •**Governing Law:** State of Texas.

**[Section 2: Facility Amount and Type]{.underline}**

> •**Type:** Senior secured revolving credit facility.
>
> •**Aggregate Commitment:** \$40,000,000.
>
> •**Current Outstanding Principal Balance** (as of March 15, 2025): \$22,500,000.
>
> •**Available Undrawn Commitment:** \$40,000,000 − \$22,500,000 = **\$17,500,000**.
>
> •**Sub-Limits:**
>
> •*Letter of Credit Sub-Facility:* \$5,000,000. No letters of credit are currently outstanding.
>
> •*Swingline Sub-Facility:* \$3,000,000.

**[Section 3: Maturity and Amortization]{.underline}**

> •**Maturity Date:** June 15, 2027 (four-year term from original execution).
>
> •**Amortization:** None. The facility is a revolving credit facility with full principal due at maturity.
>
> •**Voluntary Prepayment:** Permitted at any time without premium or penalty, subject to customary breakage costs for SOFR loans.
>
> •**Mandatory Prepayment Provisions:** See Section 6 below for detailed discussion.

**[Section 4: Interest Rate and Fees]{.underline}**

> •**Interest Rate:** At the Borrower\'s election, either (a) Term SOFR plus the applicable margin, or (b) Base Rate plus the applicable margin.
>
> •**Applicable Margin:** Based on Total Net Leverage Ratio pricing grid:

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Total Net Leverage Ratio**   **SOFR Margin**         **Base Rate Margin**
  ------------------------------ ----------------------- -----------------------
  ≤ 1.50x                        2.25%                   1.25%

  \> 1.50x but ≤ 2.50x           2.75%                   1.75%

  \> 2.50x                       3.25%                   2.25%
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

> •**Current Applicable Margin** (per most recent compliance certificate): SOFR + 2.25%. The Total Net Leverage Ratio is approximately 0.78x based on FY2024 Adjusted EBITDA.
>
> •**Commitment Fee:** 0.35% per annum on the unused portion of the commitment, payable quarterly in arrears.
>
> •**Letter of Credit Fee:** Equal to the applicable SOFR margin on the face amount of outstanding letters of credit.
>
> •**Administrative Agent Fee:** \$25,000 per annum.

**[Section 5: Security and Collateral]{.underline}**

> •**Security Interest:** First priority perfected security interest in substantially all assets of the Borrower and Guarantors, including:
>
> •Accounts receivable
>
> •Inventory
>
> •Equipment
>
> •Intellectual property (including all 14 issued U.S. utility patents and 7 pending patent applications)
>
> •General intangibles (including the Company\'s rights under the exclusive license agreement with the University of Texas at Austin for bioelectric signal processing technology, to the extent such rights are assignable as collateral)
>
> •Deposit accounts (subject to control agreements)
>
> •Investment property
>
> •**Excluded Assets:** Real property (the Company leases its principal offices); equity interests in foreign subsidiaries exceeding 65% of voting stock.
>
> •**Note:** The Tanaka Precision Components Ltd. supply agreement is listed among material contracts in the perfection certificate but is not separately pledged as collateral.

**[Section 6: Mandatory Prepayment Provisions]{.underline}**

**This section is critical for IPO due diligence and relates to the discrepancy identified in .**

**Section 2.05(b)(i) --- Equity Issuance Prepayment.** Upon consummation of any Equity Issuance (defined as any issuance of equity securities by the Borrower or any subsidiary for cash consideration, including any initial public offering), the Borrower shall prepay the Loans in an amount equal to **50% of Net Equity Proceeds in excess of \$150,000,000** received by the Borrower from such Equity Issuance. Such prepayment shall be made within five (5) business days following receipt of such Net Equity Proceeds.

\"**Net Equity Proceeds**\" is defined as: the aggregate cash proceeds received by the Borrower (not including proceeds received by any selling stockholders) from any Equity Issuance, net of (a) underwriting discounts and commissions paid by the Borrower, (b) reasonable out-of-pocket fees, costs, and expenses incurred by the Borrower in connection therewith, and (c) taxes paid or reasonably estimated to be payable in connection therewith.

**Calculation Walkthrough --- IPO Mandatory Prepayment:**

> •Gross proceeds to the Company from primary share issuance (8,000,000 shares × \$24.00 assumed IPO price) = **\$192,000,000**
>
> •Less: Underwriting discount attributable to Company shares (8,000,000 × \$1.44) = (\$11,520,000)
>
> •Net proceeds to Company from primary Firm Shares = **\$180,480,000**
>
> •Estimated offering expenses payable by the Company = **\$3,200,000**

The definition of \"Net Equity Proceeds\" permits deduction of \"reasonable out-of-pocket fees, costs, and expenses.\" Two readings are possible:

> •*If offering expenses are deductible:* \$180,480,000 − \$3,200,000 = \$177,280,000. Excess over \$150,000,000 = \$27,280,000. **50% mandatory prepayment = \$13,640,000.**
>
> •*If offering expenses are NOT deducted (conservative reading):* \$180,480,000 − \$150,000,000 = \$30,480,000. **50% mandatory prepayment = \$15,240,000.**

**KEY NOTE FOR DEAL TEAM ---** The S-1 Registration Statement (File No. 333-284517) \"Use of Proceeds\" section discloses that the Company intends to use \"approximately \$10 million\" for repayment of outstanding indebtedness under its revolving credit facility with Ridgemont National Bank. **The mandatory prepayment obligation under Section 2.05(b)(i) of the Credit Agreement, however, requires a prepayment of approximately \$15,240,000 (or \$13,640,000 if offering expenses are deducted from Net Equity Proceeds), which in either case exceeds the \$10 million disclosed in the Use of Proceeds section by a material amount (\$3,640,000 to \$5,240,000). This discrepancy should be reviewed and reconciled. The Use of Proceeds disclosure in the Final Prospectus may need to be updated to reflect the actual mandatory prepayment obligation.**

> •**Remaining balance after mandatory prepayment** (assuming \$15,240,000 prepayment): \$22,500,000 − \$15,240,000 = **\$7,260,000**.

**Greenshoe Exercise Analysis:** If the overallotment option is fully exercised, an additional 1,800,000 shares × \$22.56 net per share = \$40,608,000 additional Net Equity Proceeds to the Company, bringing total Net Equity Proceeds to approximately \$221,088,000. Excess over \$150,000,000 = \$71,088,000. 50% mandatory prepayment = \$35,544,000. This would exceed the current outstanding balance, resulting in full repayment of \$22,500,000 with no further prepayment obligation (the prepayment is capped at the outstanding balance).

**Section 2.05(b)(ii) --- Asset Disposition Prepayment.** 100% of Net Cash Proceeds from any asset disposition (other than ordinary course dispositions) exceeding \$1,000,000 individually or \$3,000,000 in the aggregate during any fiscal year.

**Section 2.05(b)(iii) --- Extraordinary Receipts.** 100% of any insurance proceeds, condemnation awards, or other extraordinary receipts exceeding \$500,000 (net of costs), to the extent not reinvested within 180 days.

**Section 2.05(b)(iv) --- Debt Issuance Prepayment.** 100% of net proceeds from any debt issuance not permitted under the Credit Agreement.

**[Section 7: Financial Covenants]{.underline}**

> •**Minimum Revenue Covenant:** Trailing twelve-month consolidated revenue of not less than \$200,000,000, tested quarterly. *Current compliance:* FY2024 revenue of \$287,400,000 --- in compliance.
>
> •**Maximum Total Net Leverage Ratio:** Total Net Leverage Ratio (defined as consolidated total funded debt minus unrestricted cash and cash equivalents, divided by consolidated Adjusted EBITDA for the trailing four fiscal quarters) not to exceed 3.00 to 1.00, tested quarterly. *Current approximate ratio:* \~0.78x --- in compliance.
>
> •**Minimum Liquidity:** Unrestricted cash and cash equivalents plus available revolving commitment of not less than \$15,000,000 at all times.
>
> •**Fixed Charge Coverage Ratio:** Not less than 1.25 to 1.00, tested quarterly.
>
> •**Post-IPO Note:** The Company is expected to remain in compliance with all financial covenants following the IPO. The mandatory prepayment will reduce funded debt, further improving the Total Net Leverage Ratio.

**[Section 8: Negative Covenants]{.underline}**

> •**Indebtedness:** No additional indebtedness except permitted indebtedness (includes capital leases up to \$5,000,000, purchase money indebtedness up to \$3,000,000, intercompany loans, and other customary baskets).
>
> •**Liens:** No liens except permitted liens (includes purchase money liens, statutory liens, existing liens identified on a schedule, and a general basket of \$2,000,000).
>
> •**Investments:** Restricted to permitted investments (includes cash equivalents, existing investments, investments in subsidiaries, and a general basket of \$5,000,000).
>
> •**Restricted Payments:** No dividends, distributions, or share repurchases except (a) tax distributions, (b) repurchases of equity from former employees/directors up to \$1,000,000 per fiscal year, and (c) so long as no Default or Event of Default exists and the Total Net Leverage Ratio is less than 1.50x (pro forma), other restricted payments up to \$3,000,000 per fiscal year. **Note:** The IPO itself is not a \"Restricted Payment\" --- proceeds flow to the Company from the primary issuance. Selling stockholder proceeds do not flow through the Company and thus are not relevant to this covenant.
>
> •**Fundamental Changes / Mergers:** No mergers, consolidations, or dissolutions except for mergers of wholly-owned subsidiaries into the Borrower or other wholly-owned subsidiaries.
>
> •**Asset Dispositions:** No dispositions of assets outside the ordinary course exceeding \$1,000,000 individually or \$3,000,000 in the aggregate per fiscal year without Lender consent.
>
> •**Transactions with Affiliates:** No transactions with affiliates except on arm\'s-length terms; transactions below \$500,000 in any fiscal year are excluded from this restriction.
>
> •**Amendment of Organizational Documents:** No material amendments to the certificate of incorporation or bylaws without prior written consent of the Lender. **Note:** The Company filed an Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation on December 20, 2024. Confirm that Ridgemont National Bank consented to such amendment. Additionally, the Company may need to file a further amended certificate in connection with the IPO; Lender consent should be confirmed.

**[Section 9: Change of Control Provisions]{.underline}**

\"**Change of Control**\" is defined as: (a) any person or group (within the meaning of Section 13(d) of the Exchange Act) acquires beneficial ownership of more than 35% of the outstanding voting equity interests of the Borrower; (b) during any 12-consecutive-month period, a majority of the Board of Directors of the Borrower ceases to consist of Continuing Directors (defined as directors who were members of the Board on the closing date of the Credit Agreement or who were nominated or appointed by a majority of the then-Continuing Directors); or (c) the Borrower ceases to own, directly or indirectly, 100% of the equity interests in each material subsidiary.

**Consequence:** A Change of Control constitutes an Event of Default, permitting the Lender to accelerate all obligations and terminate the commitments.

**IPO Analysis:** The consummation of the IPO is not expected to constitute a Change of Control. Post-IPO, no person or group is expected to beneficially own more than 35% of outstanding voting equity. Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy will hold 7,900,000 shares out of 50,000,000 post-IPO shares (15.8%) --- the largest single holder. Cascade Ridge Ventures will hold 4,200,000 shares (8.4%). Northlight Growth Partners Fund II will hold 3,300,000 shares (6.6%). The Board will continue to have a majority of Continuing Directors. However, this analysis should be re-confirmed if the final offering size or structure changes materially.

**[Section 10: Affirmative Covenants (Summary)]{.underline}**

> •Delivery of annual audited financial statements within 90 days of fiscal year-end.
>
> •Delivery of quarterly unaudited financial statements within 45 days of quarter-end.
>
> •Delivery of quarterly compliance certificates.
>
> •Maintenance of insurance.
>
> •Maintenance of properties and assets in good working order.
>
> •Compliance with laws (including FDA regulations).
>
> •Payment of taxes.
>
> •Notice of material litigation and material adverse events within 5 business days.
>
> •**Reporting Regarding IPO:** The Credit Agreement requires the Borrower to provide at least 10 business days\' prior written notice of any Equity Issuance, together with a reasonably detailed summary of the terms thereof. Confirm that this notice was timely provided to Ridgemont National Bank.
>
> •Maintenance of books and records; Lender inspection rights.
>
> •Further assurances regarding collateral.

**[Section 11: Events of Default]{.underline}**

> •Non-payment of principal, interest, or fees when due (with 3-business-day grace period for interest and fees).
>
> •Breach of financial covenants (no cure period).
>
> •Breach of other covenants (30-day cure period for curable breaches).
>
> •Cross-default to other indebtedness exceeding \$1,000,000.
>
> •Judgments in excess of \$2,000,000 not covered by insurance or stayed within 60 days.
>
> •ERISA events.
>
> •Change of Control (see Section 9 above).
>
> •Material misrepresentation.
>
> •Bankruptcy / insolvency.
>
> •Invalidity of collateral documents.

**Note Regarding VitaBand Litigation:** The pending patent infringement suit by VitaBand Corp. (Case No. 6:24-cv-00891, E.D. Tex.) seeks damages of \$45,000,000 and injunctive relief. If an adverse judgment were entered in excess of \$2,000,000 and not stayed or covered by insurance, it could constitute an Event of Default. The Company\'s litigation counsel believes the claims are without merit and no loss reserve has been established for this matter. The EEOC charge (\$350,000 reserve) is below the threshold.

**[Section 12: Representations and Warranties of the Borrower (Summary)]{.underline}**

Standard credit agreement representations including:

> •Organization, existence, and good standing.
>
> •Authorization and enforceability.
>
> •No conflicts with organizational documents, material agreements, or applicable law.
>
> •Financial statements fairly present financial condition.
>
> •No Material Adverse Effect since December 31, 2023.
>
> •Compliance with laws (including FDA regulations applicable to medical device companies).
>
> •Intellectual property --- Company owns or has valid licenses to all IP material to the business; no IP claims pending other than disclosed (VitaBand suit disclosed).
>
> •Taxes --- all material tax returns filed and taxes paid; disclosure of \$14,200,000 in federal NOL carryforwards.
>
> •Litigation --- VitaBand suit and EEOC charge disclosed.
>
> •Solvency.
>
> •Collateral --- valid first priority security interests.
>
> •ERISA compliance.
>
> •Environmental compliance.
>
> •Anti-corruption and sanctions compliance.

**[Section 13: Amendment and Waiver Provisions]{.underline}**

> •Amendments and waivers require written consent of both the Borrower and the Lender.
>
> •No oral modifications.
>
> •Waivers are limited to the specific instances granted and do not constitute continuing waivers.

**[Section 14: Miscellaneous Terms]{.underline}**

> •**Governing Law:** State of Texas.
>
> •**Jurisdiction:** State and federal courts located in Travis County, Texas.
>
> •**Notices to Borrower:** 4200 Clearwater Blvd., Suite 800, Austin, TX 78759, Attention: David Nishimura, Chief Financial Officer, with a copy to Samantha Reeves, General Counsel.
>
> •**Notices to Lender:** Ridgemont National Bank (address provided in Credit Agreement).
>
> •**Assignments:** Lender may assign its interests without restriction; Borrower may not assign without prior Lender consent.
>
> •**Confidentiality:** Standard confidentiality provisions regarding Borrower information.
>
> •**Waiver of Jury Trial:** Both parties waive the right to jury trial.

**[Section 15: Deal Team Notes and Open Items]{.underline}**

The following items have been identified during due diligence review of the Credit Agreement in connection with the IPO and require resolution prior to the effectiveness of the Registration Statement or the closing of the offering:

**1. Mandatory Prepayment Discrepancy .** The mandatory prepayment obligation under Section 2.05(b)(i) of the Credit Agreement requires the Borrower to prepay an amount equal to 50% of Net Equity Proceeds in excess of \$150,000,000. Based on assumed IPO pricing, this yields a mandatory prepayment of **\$15,240,000** (or **\$13,640,000** if offering expenses of \$3,200,000 are deductible from Net Equity Proceeds). The S-1 Registration Statement (File No. 333-284517) Use of Proceeds section discloses only \"approximately \$10 million\" for repayment of revolving credit indebtedness. **This understates the mandatory prepayment obligation by \$3,640,000 to \$5,240,000.** Recommendations: (a) the Use of Proceeds section of the Final Prospectus must be revised to accurately reflect the mandatory prepayment obligation; (b) Company counsel should confirm the exact interpretation of \"Net Equity Proceeds\" and whether offering expenses are deductible; and (c) the underwriting agreement representations regarding material contracts and use of proceeds must account for this obligation. **Failure to reconcile this discrepancy may result in a material misstatement in the Registration Statement.**

**2. Lender Consent for Organizational Document Amendments.** Confirm that Ridgemont National Bank provided written consent for the Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation filed December 20, 2024. Confirm whether any additional amendments to organizational documents in connection with the IPO require Lender consent.

**3. Equity Issuance Prior Notice Requirement.** Confirm that the required 10-business-day advance written notice of the IPO (as an Equity Issuance) was timely provided to Ridgemont National Bank, together with a reasonably detailed summary of the offering terms.

**4. Change of Control Analysis.** Confirm that the post-IPO ownership structure does not trigger a Change of Control under the Credit Agreement. Note that if any investor were to accumulate shares exceeding 35% of outstanding voting equity post-IPO, this could trigger acceleration of all obligations.

**5. Collateral --- IP Security Interest and UT Austin License.** The security interest covers intellectual property and general intangibles, including the Company\'s rights under the exclusive license agreement with the University of Texas at Austin, to the extent assignable. Flag potential interaction with UT license anti-assignment or change-of-control provisions. *Cross-reference:* The UT license should also be reviewed for restrictions on granting security interests in connection with the underwriting agreement analysis (see in the underwriting agreement review memorandum).

**6. Post-Prepayment Facility Availability.** After a mandatory prepayment of \$15,240,000, the outstanding balance would be reduced to \$7,260,000, with \$32,740,000 available under the revolving facility. This provides the Company with significant liquidity post-IPO.

*Prepared by Stonebridge & Calloway LLP. For internal deal team use only. Not for distribution outside the working group. Last updated: March 2025.*
