**[POWER OF ATTORNEY AND CUSTODY AGREEMENT]{.underline}**

**Relating to the Initial Public Offering of Common Stock of Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc.**

**Dated as of March \_\_, 2025**

This Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement (this \"**Agreement**\") is a form document to be executed individually by each Selling Stockholder identified herein in connection with the initial public offering of common stock of Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc. This Agreement is expected to be executed on or prior to the Pricing Date of March 19, 2025.

**[RECITALS]{.underline}**

**WHEREAS**, Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc., a Delaware corporation (the \"**Company**\"), with principal offices at 4200 Clearwater Blvd., Suite 800, Austin, Texas 78759, proposes to file a Registration Statement on Form S-1 (File No. 333-284517) with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the \"**Commission**\") under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the \"**Securities Act**\"), relating to the initial public offering (the \"**Offering**\") of shares of the Company\'s common stock, par value \$0.001 per share (the \"**Common Stock**\");

**WHEREAS**, the Offering will include the sale of shares of Common Stock by certain stockholders of the Company (each, a \"**Selling Stockholder**\"), as described in the Registration Statement and the related Prospectus;

**WHEREAS**, the undersigned Selling Stockholder (the \"**Stockholder**\") desires to sell shares of Common Stock in the Offering and wishes to appoint attorneys-in-fact and a custodian to act on the Stockholder\'s behalf in connection therewith;

**WHEREAS**, the underwriters of the Offering are expected to be led by Hargrove Securities LLC, as lead book-running manager, and Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc., as co-manager (collectively, the \"**Underwriters**\");

**WHEREAS**, the Stockholder desires to irrevocably appoint David Nishimura, Chief Financial Officer of the Company, and Samantha Reeves, General Counsel and Secretary of the Company, as the Stockholder\'s attorneys-in-fact and custodians for the purposes set forth herein; and

**WHEREAS, the Selling Stockholders consist of Cascade Kestridge Ventures, LP, a Delaware limited partnership, Northlight Growth Partners Fund II, LP, a Delaware limited partnership, and Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy, an individual, each of whom executes a separate copy of this Agreement.**

**NOW, THEREFORE**, in consideration of the premises and the mutual covenants and agreements contained herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the Stockholder hereby agrees as follows:

**[1. Appointment of Attorneys-in-Fact]{.underline}**

The Stockholder hereby irrevocably constitutes and appoints David Nishimura and Samantha Reeves, and each of them individually (the \"**Attorneys-in-Fact**\"), as the Stockholder\'s true and lawful attorneys-in-fact, with full power of substitution and resubstitution, for the Stockholder and in the Stockholder\'s name, place, and stead, to act on behalf of the Stockholder in connection with the Offering, with full power and authority to do and perform each and every act and thing requisite and necessary to be done in connection therewith, as fully and to all intents and purposes as the Stockholder might or could do in person.

This appointment constitutes a durable power of attorney coupled with an interest and shall not be revoked by the death, disability, or incapacity of the Stockholder, or, in the case of an entity, the dissolution or termination of the Stockholder. This appointment is irrevocable and shall survive and remain in full force and effect until the earlier of: (a) the completion of the Closing (and any Additional Closing relating to the exercise of the over-allotment option) under the Underwriting Agreement; or (b) the termination of the Underwriting Agreement without any sale of shares having occurred.

**[2. Scope of Authority]{.underline}**

The Stockholder hereby grants to the Attorneys-in-Fact full power and authority to take any and all of the following actions on behalf of and in the name of the Stockholder:

> \(a\) Execute and deliver, on behalf of and in the name of the Stockholder, the underwriting agreement (the \"**Underwriting Agreement**\") among the Company, the Selling Stockholders, and the Underwriters, in such form as the Attorneys-in-Fact shall approve;
>
> \(b\) Execute and deliver any amendments, supplements, or modifications to the Underwriting Agreement;
>
> \(c\) Execute and deliver any lock-up agreement or market standoff agreement required by the Underwriters in connection with the Offering, including a lock-up agreement providing for a 180-day restricted period from the date of the Final Prospectus (and, in the case of Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy, inclusive of the springing extension provision described in the applicable term sheet);
>
> \(d\) Deliver or cause to be delivered the shares of Common Stock to be sold by the Stockholder (the \"**Stockholder Shares**\") pursuant to the Underwriting Agreement, including by directing the Transfer Agent, Atlantic Stock Transfer & Trust Company, to transfer such shares in accordance with the terms of the Underwriting Agreement;
>
> \(e\) Receive and receipt for the net proceeds from the sale of the Stockholder Shares, after deduction of the underwriting discount and any applicable transfer taxes, and deposit such proceeds in accordance with the Stockholder\'s written instructions;
>
> \(f\) Execute and deliver selling stockholder questionnaires, certificates, and other documents required in connection with the Registration Statement, the Underwriting Agreement, or the Offering;
>
> \(g\) Furnish information in writing on behalf of the Stockholder for inclusion in the Registration Statement and Prospectus, subject to the Stockholder\'s prior written approval of the specific information to be furnished;
>
> \(h\) Determine the number of Stockholder Shares to be sold in the Offering, consistent with the maximum number specified in Schedule A hereto;
>
> \(i\) Execute, deliver, and file any documents required in connection with applicable federal, state, or foreign securities laws; and
>
> \(j\) Take all other actions and execute all other documents as may be necessary or desirable to consummate the sale of the Stockholder Shares in the Offering.

**[3. Custody of Shares]{.underline}**

The Stockholder hereby deposits with the Attorneys-in-Fact, acting as custodians (in such capacity, the \"**Custodians**\"), certificates or book-entry positions representing the Stockholder Shares, or agrees to cause such shares to be delivered to or held by the Custodians or the Transfer Agent (Atlantic Stock Transfer & Trust Company) for the benefit of the Custodians, promptly following execution of this Agreement.

The Custodians shall hold the Stockholder Shares in custody for the purposes of delivery to the Underwriters at the Closing (expected to be March 24, 2025, or such other date as determined pursuant to the Underwriting Agreement) and any Additional Closing related to the exercise of the over-allotment option.

If the Stockholder Shares are held in certificated form, the Stockholder shall deliver stock certificates endorsed in blank or accompanied by stock powers duly executed in blank, with signatures guaranteed, to the Custodians. If the Stockholder Shares are held in book-entry form, the Stockholder shall cause Atlantic Stock Transfer & Trust Company to hold such shares subject to the instructions of the Custodians.

The Custodians shall not release or deliver the Stockholder Shares except: (i) to the Underwriters at the Closing or any Additional Closing pursuant to the Underwriting Agreement; (ii) back to the Stockholder if the Underwriting Agreement is terminated prior to any Closing without any sale of shares having occurred; or (iii) as otherwise directed by the Stockholder and the Attorneys-in-Fact jointly in writing.

**[4. Number of Shares; Schedule A]{.underline}**

The maximum number of Stockholder Shares to be sold by the Stockholder in the Offering is as set forth on **Schedule A** attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference. The specific number of shares to be sold, the public offering price per share, the underwriting discount per share, and the net proceeds to the Stockholder per share shall be as set forth in the Underwriting Agreement as executed by the Attorneys-in-Fact on behalf of the Stockholder.

The Stockholder hereby authorizes the Attorneys-in-Fact to agree to a reduction, but not an increase, of the number of Stockholder Shares from the maximum number set forth on Schedule A, if necessary or appropriate in connection with the Offering. Schedule A sets forth the share information for each Selling Stockholder: Cascade Kestridge Ventures, LP --- up to 2,000,000 shares; Northlight Growth Partners Fund II, LP --- up to 1,500,000 shares; and Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy --- up to 500,000 shares.

**[5. Distribution of Proceeds]{.underline}**

Upon receipt of the net proceeds from the sale of the Stockholder Shares (calculated as the public offering price per share less the underwriting discount per share of \$1.44, less any applicable transfer taxes), the Custodians shall distribute such proceeds to the Stockholder in accordance with the Stockholder\'s written wire transfer instructions provided to the Custodians prior to the Closing Date.

The Custodians shall deduct from the Stockholder\'s net proceeds any transfer taxes, if any, payable by the Stockholder in connection with the sale of the Stockholder Shares; provided, however, that the Company has agreed, pursuant to the terms of the Offering, to reimburse transfer taxes attributable to Selling Stockholder shares, if any.

The Custodians shall have no obligation to invest or reinvest any proceeds held temporarily pending distribution to the Stockholder and shall not be liable for any interest or earnings on proceeds so held.

**[6. Representations and Warranties of the Stockholder]{.underline}**

The Stockholder hereby represents and warrants to the Attorneys-in-Fact and the Company as follows:

> \(a\) **Authority.** The Stockholder has full right, power, and authority to enter into this Agreement and to sell, assign, transfer, and deliver the Stockholder Shares. If the Stockholder is an entity, it is duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of its jurisdiction of organization, and the execution and delivery of this Agreement and the consummation of the transactions contemplated hereby have been duly authorized by all necessary action on the part of the Stockholder.
>
> \(b\) **Title to Shares.** The Stockholder is the record and beneficial owner of the shares of Common Stock set forth on Schedule A, free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, equities, and claims, and has full right and authority to sell and deliver such shares. Upon delivery of and payment for the Stockholder Shares pursuant to the Underwriting Agreement, the Underwriters will receive valid title to such shares, free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, equities, and claims.
>
> \(c\) **No Conflicts.** The execution and delivery of this Agreement and the sale of the Stockholder Shares will not conflict with, result in a breach of, or constitute a default under: (i) any agreement, indenture, or instrument to which the Stockholder is a party or by which the Stockholder is bound; (ii) the organizational documents of the Stockholder (if an entity); or (iii) any order, judgment, or decree of any court or governmental authority having jurisdiction over the Stockholder.
>
> \(d\) **No Registration Rights Violation.** The sale of the Stockholder Shares does not and will not violate any registration rights agreement or similar agreement to which the Stockholder is a party.
>
> \(e\) **Selling Stockholder Information.** All information furnished or to be furnished by the Stockholder in writing expressly for use in the Registration Statement (File No. 333-284517), the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto is and will be true, correct, and complete in all material respects and does not and will not contain any untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading.
>
> \(f\) **No Manipulation.** The Stockholder has not taken and will not take, directly or indirectly, any action designed to, or that might reasonably be expected to, cause or result in stabilization or manipulation of the price of the Common Stock in violation of applicable law.
>
> \(g\) **Compliance with Lock-Up.** The Stockholder will execute and deliver a lock-up agreement in the form required by the lead Underwriter, Hargrove Securities LLC, and will comply with all restrictions on transfer set forth therein for the duration of the applicable restricted period.
>
> \(h\) **No Brokers.** No broker, finder, or similar agent has been employed by or on behalf of the Stockholder in connection with the Offering, and no person is entitled to any commission, fee, or other compensation from the Stockholder in connection with the sale of the Stockholder Shares, other than the underwriting discount payable to the Underwriters.

**[7. Duties and Liabilities of Attorneys-in-Fact and Custodians]{.underline}**

The Attorneys-in-Fact and Custodians shall not be liable for any action taken or omitted to be taken in good faith in connection with this Agreement, except for their own willful misconduct or gross negligence. The Attorneys-in-Fact and Custodians are acting solely as agents of the Stockholder and shall have no fiduciary or other duty to the Stockholder beyond the specific duties expressly set forth in this Agreement. The Attorneys-in-Fact shall act in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and the Underwriting Agreement.

The Stockholder acknowledges that David Nishimura serves as Chief Financial Officer and Samantha Reeves serves as General Counsel and Secretary of the Company, and that each is also acting on behalf of the Company and other Selling Stockholders in connection with the Offering. The Stockholder hereby waives any conflict of interest arising therefrom. The Attorneys-in-Fact shall have no obligation to take any action that they reasonably believe to be contrary to applicable law.

**[8. Indemnification of Attorneys-in-Fact and Custodians]{.underline}**

The Stockholder agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Attorneys-in-Fact and Custodians from and against any and all losses, claims, damages, liabilities, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees and expenses) arising out of or relating to the performance of their duties under this Agreement, except to the extent that such losses, claims, damages, liabilities, or expenses result from the willful misconduct or gross negligence of the Attorneys-in-Fact or Custodians. This indemnification obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement.

**[9. Irrevocability]{.underline}**

This Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement is coupled with an interest and is irrevocable. It shall not be revoked by the death, disability, incapacity, dissolution, liquidation, or termination of the Stockholder, or by any other event. This Agreement may be terminated only upon mutual written agreement of the Stockholder and the Attorneys-in-Fact, or automatically upon the earlier of: (a) the completion of all closings under the Underwriting Agreement, including any closing related to the exercise of the over-allotment option (the deadline for exercise of which is April 23, 2025); or (b) the termination of the Underwriting Agreement without any closing having occurred.

**[10. Successors and Assigns]{.underline}**

This Agreement shall be binding upon the Stockholder and the Stockholder\'s heirs, executors, administrators, legal representatives, successors, and assigns. This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of the Attorneys-in-Fact and their successors. The Attorneys-in-Fact may not assign their obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the Stockholder; provided, however, that the Company may designate a successor to serve in the capacity of Attorney-in-Fact if either David Nishimura or Samantha Reeves ceases to be an officer of the Company, upon written notice to the Stockholder.

**[11. Governing Law]{.underline}**

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to conflict-of-laws principles thereof. Any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the federal and state courts located in the Borough of Manhattan, City of New York, and each party hereby irrevocably consents to the personal jurisdiction of such courts.

**[12. Notices]{.underline}**

All notices, requests, demands, and other communications under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered to the parties at the following addresses:

> If to the Attorneys-in-Fact / Custodians:
>
> c/o Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc. 4200 Clearwater Blvd., Suite 800 Austin, Texas 78759
>
> Attention: David Nishimura, Chief Financial Officer
>
> and Samantha Reeves, General Counsel
>
> If to the Stockholder:
>
> At the address set forth on the signature page hereto.

Notices shall be deemed given upon personal delivery, one (1) business day after deposit with a nationally recognized overnight courier service, or three (3) business days after mailing by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid.

**[13. Miscellaneous]{.underline}**

**Entire Agreement.** This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations, and discussions, whether oral or written, relating to such subject matter.

**Amendments.** This Agreement may not be amended, modified, or supplemented except by a written instrument signed by the Stockholder and the Attorneys-in-Fact.

**Severability.** If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable in any respect, such invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability shall not affect any other provision hereof, and the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.

**Counterparts.** This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Electronic signatures and PDF copies of executed counterparts shall be treated as originals for all purposes.

**Headings.** Section headings contained in this Agreement are for convenience of reference only and shall not affect the meaning or interpretation of this Agreement.

**Third-Party Beneficiaries.** The Underwriters, Hargrove Securities LLC and Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc., are intended third-party beneficiaries of this Agreement with respect to the obligation of the Stockholder to deliver the Stockholder Shares and the authority of the Attorneys-in-Fact to execute the Underwriting Agreement on behalf of the Stockholder.

**\[SIGNATURE PAGES FOLLOW\]**

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)

"""Validate a .docx for delivery.

Checks ZIP integrity, XML well-formedness for every part, [Content_Types].xml
presence, and relationship consistency (no dangling rIds). Does NOT do XSD
schema validation in v1 — defer until ECMA-376 XSDs are vendored fresh from
ECMA-International.

Usage: python validate.py file.docx
Exit 0 = valid; non-zero = errors printed to stderr.
"""
import sys
import zipfile
from pathlib import Path
from lxml import etree


def validate(path: Path) -> list[str]:
    errors = []

    if not path.exists():
        return [f"File not found: {path}"]
    if not zipfile.is_zipfile(path):
        return [f"Not a valid ZIP: {path}"]

    with zipfile.ZipFile(path) as z:
        names = set(z.namelist())

        if "[Content_Types].xml" not in names:
            errors.append("Missing [Content_Types].xml")
            return errors

        # XML well-formedness for every .xml and .rels part
        for name in names:
            if name.endswith(".xml") or name.ends

# UNDERWRITING AGREEMENT

**Firm-Commitment Underwriting Agreement**

**Dated as of March 19, 2025**

**Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc.**

**Cascade Kestridge Ventures, LP**

**Northlight Growth Partners Fund II, LP**

**Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy**

**and**

**Hargrove Securities LLC**

**and**

\newpage

# UNDERWRITING AGREEMENT

This Underwriting Agreement (this "Agreement") is dated as of March 19, 2025 (the "Date of this Agreement" or the "Pricing Date"), by and among **Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc.**, a corporation duly organized and existing under the laws of the State of Delaware (the "Company"), the selling stockholders named in **Schedule II** hereto (collectively, the "Selling Stockholders" and each, a "Selling Stockholder"), **Hargrove Securities LLC**, a limited liability company organized under the laws of the State of New York (the "Representative"), acting on behalf of the several Underwriters named in **Schedule I** hereto, and **Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc.**, a corporation organized under the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts. The Representative and Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc. are referred to herein collectively as the "Underwriters" and each as an "Underwriter."

The Company and the Selling Stockholders propose to issue and sell, and the Underwriters propose to purchase, severally and not jointly, the shares of common stock, par value \$0.001 per share, of the Company (the "Common Stock") described in this Agreement. The public offering price per share of Common Stock is \$24.00 (the "Public Offering Price"), which represents the midpoint of the estimated offering price range of \$22.00 to \$26.00 per share set forth on the cover page of the Preliminary Prospectus (as defined below). The final Public Offering Price shall be confirmed by the parties on the Pricing Date and, if different from \$24.00, the per-share and aggregate amounts set forth in this Agreement and the Schedules hereto shall be adjusted accordingly.

The Company has prepared and filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "Commission" or the "SEC") a registration statement on Form S-1 (File No. 333-284517) (the "Registration Statement") for the registration of the Shares (as defined below) under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "Act"). The Registration Statement was initially filed with the Commission on January 15, 2025; Amendment No. 1 was filed on February 10, 2025; Amendment No. 2 was filed on March 3, 2025; and the Registration Statement was declared effective by the Commission on March 12, 2025. The prospectus dated March 12, 2025, included in the Registration Statement at the time it became effective, is referred to herein as the "Preliminary Prospectus." The final prospectus relating to the Shares, in the form first filed with the Commission pursuant to Rule 424(b)(4) under the Act, is referred to herein as the "Final Prospectus."

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements herein contained, the parties hereto agree as follows:

\newpage

## ARTICLE I — REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES OF THE COMPANY

The Company represents and warrants to each Underwriter, as of the Date of this Agreement and as of the Closing Date (as defined below), as follows:

**1.1 Organization, Good Standing, and Corporate Power.** The Company is a corporation duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of the State of Delaware and has full corporate power and authority to own, lease, and operate its properties and to carry on its business as presently conducted and as proposed to be conducted, and is duly qualified to do business as a foreign corporation in each jurisdiction in which the nature of its business or the ownership or leasing of its properties makes such qualification necessary, except where the failure to be so qualified would not have a Material Adverse Effect (as defined in Section 1.10). The Company has no material subsidiaries other than as disclosed in the Pricing Disclosure Package (as defined in Section 1.6).

**1.2 Authorization; Enforceability.** The Company has full corporate power and authority to execute and deliver this Agreement and to perform its obligations hereunder. This Agreement has been duly authorized, executed, and delivered by the Company and constitutes the legal, valid, and binding obligation of the Company, enforceable against the Company in accordance with its terms, except (a) as enforceability may be limited by applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization, moratorium, or similar laws affecting creditors' rights generally and by general principles of equity and (b) as indemnification and contribution provisions may be limited by applicable law or public policy.

**1.3 Capitalization.** The authorized capital stock of the Company consists of 200,000,000 shares of Common Stock, par value \$0.001 per share, and 10,000,000 shares of preferred stock, par value \$0.001 per share. As of the close of business on the Date of this Agreement, 42,000,000 shares of Common Stock are issued and outstanding, and no shares of preferred stock are issued and outstanding. All of the outstanding shares of Common Stock have been duly authorized, validly issued, fully paid, and nonassessable, and were issued in compliance with applicable federal and state securities laws. The Company's amended and restated certificate of incorporation, effective as of December 20, 2024, and its amended and restated bylaws are in the form filed as exhibits to the Registration Statement. Except as disclosed in the Pricing Disclosure Package, there are no outstanding options, warrants, or other rights to purchase or acquire any shares of capital stock of the Company, other than (a) 4,200,000 shares of Common Stock issuable upon the exercise of outstanding stock options under the Company's 2024 Equity Incentive Plan at a weighted-average exercise price of \$6.50 per share and (b) 1,800,000 shares of Common Stock reserved for future grants under the 2024 Equity Incentive Plan.

**1.4 Validity of the Shares.** The Firm Shares (as defined in Section 3.1) and the Option Shares (as defined in Section 4.1), when issued and delivered by the Company against payment of the purchase price therefor in accordance with this Agreement, will be duly authorized, validly issued, fully paid, and nonassessable, and will be issued in compliance with applicable federal and state securities laws.

**1.5 No Conflicts.** The execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement by the Company, the issuance and sale of the Shares, and the consummation of the transactions contemplated hereby will not (a) conflict with or result in a breach or violation of, or constitute a default under, the Company's amended and restated certificate of incorporation or amended and restated bylaws; (b) conflict with or result in a breach or violation of, or constitute a default under, any material contract or instrument to which the Company is a party or by which it or any of its properties is bound; or (c) conflict with or result in a violation of any applicable law, rule, or regulation, in each case except where such conflict, breach, violation, or default would not have a Material Adverse Effect.

**1.6 Registration Statement and Prospectus.** The Registration Statement, at the time it became effective, met in all material respects the requirements of the Act and the rules and regulations of the Commission thereunder and did not contain an untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein not misleading. The Preliminary Prospectus, at the time of the Date of this Agreement, did not and does not contain an untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact necessary in order to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading. The term "Pricing Disclosure Package" means the Preliminary Prospectus, together with any Issuer Free Writing Prospectus (as defined in Section 1.7) and any other information permitted to be omitted therefrom under Rule 430A or Rule 434 under the Act, in each case as of the Date of this Agreement. The Final Prospectus, at the time it is filed with the Commission pursuant to Rule 424(b)(4) under the Act, will not contain an untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact necessary in order to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading. The foregoing representations and warranties in this Section 1.6 do not apply to statements in or omissions from the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Pricing Disclosure Package, or the Final Prospectus made in reliance upon and in conformity with written information furnished to the Company by or on behalf of any Underwriter specifically for use therein.

**1.7 Free Writing Prospectuses.** The Company has not, and will not, without the prior written consent of the Representative, make any offer to sell the Shares that would constitute an "issuer free writing prospectus" (as defined in Rule 433 under the Act) or that would otherwise constitute a "free writing prospectus" (as defined in Rule 405 under the Act). The Company has not distributed and will not distribute, prior to the Closing Date, any "written communication" (as defined in Rule 405 under the Act), other than the Preliminary Prospectus, the Final Prospectus, and any Issuer Free Writing Prospectus consented to by the Representative and the Company.

**1.8 Financial Statements.** The financial statements (including the related notes) included in the Registration Statement and the Pricing Disclosure Package present fairly, in all material respects, the financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows of the Company as of the dates and for the periods indicated, and have been prepared in conformity with generally accepted accounting principles in the United States ("GAAP") consistently applied throughout the periods involved, and have been audited by Whitman Reese & Co., an independent registered public accounting firm, in accordance with the standards of the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board (the "PCAOB").

**1.9 No Material Adverse Change.** Since the date of the most recent audited financial statements included in the Registration Statement (December 31, 2024), (a) there has been no material adverse change in the business, properties, management, financial condition, or results of operations of the Company, and (b) the Company has not incurred any material liabilities or obligations, direct or contingent, not disclosed in the Pricing Disclosure Package, except as disclosed in the Pricing Disclosure Package.

**1.10 Definition of Material Adverse Effect.** As used in this Agreement, "Material Adverse Effect" means any event, circumstance, change, or effect that, individually or in the aggregate, has had or would reasonably be expected to have a material adverse effect on the business, properties, financial condition, results of operations, or prospects of the Company and its subsidiaries, taken as a whole.

**1.11 Intellectual Property.** The Company owns or possesses sufficient legal rights to all patents, patent applications, trademarks, service marks, trade names, copyrights, domain names, trade secrets, know-how, and other intellectual property necessary for the conduct of its business as presently conducted and as proposed to be conducted, without any known infringement of the rights of others, except where the failure to own or possess such rights would not have a Material Adverse Effect. The Company holds 14 issued U.S. utility patents and has 7 pending patent applications, and is a party to an exclusive license agreement with the University of Texas at Austin for certain bioelectric signal processing technology incorporated into the Company's PulseGuard Pro device. The Company has not received any written notice of infringement, conflict, or claim alleging that the Company's products or processes infringe the intellectual property rights of any other person, except as disclosed in the Pricing Disclosure Package, including the patent infringement action brought by VitaBand Corp. (Case No. 6:24-cv-00891, U.S. District Court, Eastern District of Texas).

**1.12 Material Contracts.** Each material contract to which the Company is a party is in full force and effect, constitutes a legal, valid, and binding obligation of the Company enforceable against it in accordance with its terms, and the Company is not in material breach or default under any such contract. The material contracts of the Company include (a) the supply agreement with Tanaka Precision Components Ltd., effective through December 31, 2027, including a minimum annual purchase commitment of \$12,000,000; (b) the exclusive license agreement with the University of Texas at Austin, effective July 1, 2018, with a remaining term through June 30, 2033, including a royalty of 3.5% of net sales and a minimum annual royalty of \$500,000; and (c) the revolving credit facility with Oakvale National Bank, in an aggregate principal amount of up to \$40,000,000, of which \$22,500,000 was outstanding as of December 31, 2024.

**1.13 Regulatory Compliance; FDA.** The Company is in compliance in all material respects with all applicable laws, rules, and regulations, including applicable regulations of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (the "FDA"). The Company's PulseGuard Pro cardiac rhythm monitoring device received FDA 510(k) clearance (Clearance No. K230847) on August 22, 2023, and the Company is in compliance in all material respects with all applicable post-market surveillance, quality system, and medical device reporting requirements.

**1.14 Litigation.** Except as disclosed in the Pricing Disclosure Package, there is no action, suit, proceeding, or investigation pending or, to the knowledge of the Company, threatened against the Company before any court or governmental authority that, if adversely determined, would have a Material Adverse Effect. The matters disclosed in the Pricing Disclosure Package include (a) the patent infringement action filed by VitaBand Corp. on June 14, 2024 (Case No. 6:24-cv-00891, U.S. District Court, Eastern District of Texas), seeking monetary damages of \$45,000,000, enhanced damages, attorneys' fees, costs, and permanent injunctive relief, and (b) a charge of discrimination filed with the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission on September 3, 2024, alleging age discrimination, with respect to which the Company has established a reserve of \$350,000.

**1.15 Tax Matters.** Company has filed all material federal, state, and local tax returns required to be filed and has paid all material taxes due, except for taxes that are being contested in good faith by appropriate proceedings and for which adequate reserves have been established. As of December 31, 2024, the Company had approximately \$14.2 million in federal net operating loss carryforwards. The Company has completed a Section 382 study through December 31, 2024, and has determined that no ownership change has occurred through that date that would limit the Company's ability to utilize such net operating loss carryforwards.

**1.16 FINRA Affiliations.** No officer, director, or beneficial owner of 5% or more of the Company's outstanding Common Stock has any direct or indirect affiliation or association with any Underwriter or any person who controls any Underwriter, except as disclosed in the Pricing Disclosure Package and except for any relationship that would not require the offering to be subject to FINRA Rule 5121.

**1.17 Securities Law Compliance.** The Company has complied and will comply in all material respects with all applicable federal and state securities laws in connection with the offering and sale of the Shares.

**1.18 Nasdaq Listing.** The Shares have been approved for listing on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol "MPLS," subject to official notice of issuance, and the Company satisfies the applicable listing requirements of the Nasdaq Global Select Market.

**1.19 Use of Proceeds.** The net proceeds to the Company from the sale of the Firm Shares and the Option Shares, and the application thereof as described under the caption "Use of Proceeds" in the Preliminary Prospectus and the Final Prospectus, will be accurately described in all material respects in the Final Prospectus, including the repayment of indebtedness under the Company's revolving credit facility in accordance with the mandatory prepayment provisions of the credit agreement governing such facility.

**1.20 Independent Auditor.** Whitman Reese & Co., which has certified certain financial statements of the Company included in the Registration Statement, is an independent registered public accounting firm within the meaning of the Act and the rules and regulations of the Commission and the PCAOB.

\newpage

## ARTICLE II — REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES OF THE SELLING STOCKHOLDERS

Each Selling Stockholder, severally and not jointly, represents and warrants to each Underwriter, as of the Date of this Agreement and as of the Closing Date, as follows:

**2.1 Title to Shares.** Such Selling Stockholder is the record and beneficial owner of the Selling Stockholder Shares (as defined in Section 3.1) set forth opposite its name in Schedule II, free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, equities, claims, and security interests of any kind. Upon delivery of and payment for the Selling Stockholder Shares pursuant to this Agreement, the Underwriters will receive good and marketable title to such shares, free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, equities, claims, and security interests of any kind.

**2.2 Authorization; Enforceability.** Such Selling Stockholder has full power and authority to execute and deliver this Agreement (including through its attorneys-in-fact pursuant to the Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement) and to perform its obligations hereunder. This Agreement has been duly authorized, executed, and delivered by such Selling Stockholder and constitutes the legal, valid, and binding obligation of such Selling Stockholder, enforceable against it in accordance with its terms, except as enforceability may be limited by applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization, moratorium, or similar laws affecting creditors' rights generally and by general principles of equity.

**2.3 No Conflicts.** The execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement by such Selling Stockholder, and the sale of the Selling Stockholder Shares, will not (a) conflict with or result in a breach or violation of, or constitute a default under, the organizational documents of such Selling Stockholder (if an entity); (b) conflict with or result in a breach or violation of, or constitute a default under, any material agreement or instrument to which such Selling Stockholder is a party or by which it is bound; or (c) conflict with or result in a violation of any applicable law, rule, or regulation applicable to such Selling Stockholder.

**2.4 Selling Stockholder Information.** The information furnished in writing by such Selling Stockholder specifically for inclusion in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Pricing Disclosure Package, or the Final Prospectus (as set forth in the selling stockholder questionnaire completed by such Selling Stockholder) is and will be true, correct, and complete in all material respects and does not and will not contain an untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact necessary in order to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading.

**2.5 Securities Law Compliance.** Such Selling Stockholder has complied and will comply in all material respects with all applicable federal and state securities laws in connection with the offer and sale of the Selling Stockholder Shares.

**2.6 Power of Attorney and Custody.** Such Selling Stockholder has duly executed and delivered the Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement appointing David Nishimura and Samantha Reeves as its attorneys-in-fact and custodians, and such Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement is in full force and effect and constitutes the legal, valid, and binding obligation of such Selling Stockholder, enforceable in accordance with its terms.

**2.7 Lock-Up Agreement.** Such Selling Stockholder has duly executed and delivered a Lock-Up Agreement in the form attached as Exhibit A hereto (and, in the case of Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy, including the Springing Extension Annex attached as Exhibit A-1 hereto), and such Lock-Up Agreement is in full force and effect.

\newpage

## ARTICLE III — SALE AND DELIVERY OF SHARES BY THE COMPANY AND THE SELLING STOCKHOLDERS; PURCHASE BY THE UNDERWRITERS

**3.1 Firm Shares.** Upon the terms and subject to the conditions herein set forth, the Company agrees to issue and sell to the several Underwriters, and each Underwriter agrees, severally and not jointly, to purchase from the Company, 8,000,000 newly issued shares of Common Stock (the "Company Shares"), and the Selling Stockholders agree to sell to the several Underwriters, and each Underwriter agrees, severally and not jointly, to purchase from the Selling Stockholders, an aggregate of 4,000,000 shares of Common Stock (the "Selling Stockholder Shares"), in the respective numbers set forth opposite each Underwriter's name in Schedule I and each Selling Stockholder's name in Schedule II. The Company Shares and the Selling Stockholder Shares are collectively referred to herein as the "Firm Shares," and the Firm Shares, together with the Option Shares (as defined in Section 4.1), are referred to herein as the "Shares."

**3.2 Purchase Price.** The purchase price per share to be paid by the Underwriters to the Company and the Selling Stockholders for the Firm Shares shall be \$22.56 per share, equal to the Public Offering Price of \$24.00 per share less the underwriting discount of \$1.44 per share (equal to 6.0% of the Public Offering Price) (the "Underwriting Discount"). The aggregate purchase price for the Firm Shares shall be \$270,720,000, of which \$180,480,000 is payable to the Company for the Company Shares and \$90,240,000 is payable to the Selling Stockholders for the Selling Stockholder Shares, in each case as set forth in Schedule II.

**3.3 Several Obligations.** The obligations of the Underwriters under this Agreement are several and not joint. If any Underwriter fails to purchase the Firm Shares that it has agreed to purchase hereunder, the Representative may arrange for another party or parties to purchase such Firm Shares, and if the Representative does not so arrange, the Representative and the other Underwriters shall be entitled to the remedies set forth in Section 5.4.

\newpage

## ARTICLE IV — OVER-ALLOTMENT OPTION

**4.1 Grant of Option.** In order to facilitate the Underwriters' stabilization and covering transactions in connection with the offering, the Company hereby grants to the Underwriters, acting through the Representative, an option (the "Over-Allotment Option") to purchase, severally and not jointly, up to 1,800,000 additional shares of Common Stock (the "Option Shares"), equal to 15% of the Firm Shares, solely to cover over-allotments, if any. The Option Shares shall be newly issued shares of Common Stock issued by the Company. The Over-Allotment Option is exercisable in whole or in part at any time, and from time to time, within 30 calendar days after the Closing Date (i.e., on or before April 23, 2025).

**4.2 Exercise and Allocation.** Upon exercise of the Over-Allotment Option, the Underwriters shall be obligated to purchase the number of Option Shares specified by the Representative, allocated among the Underwriters in the same proportions as their respective allocations of the Firm Shares set forth in Schedule I (Hargrove Securities LLC: 70%, or 1,260,000 Option Shares; Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc.: 30%, or 540,000 Option Shares). The purchase price per Option Share shall be \$22.56, equal to the Public Offering Price less the Underwriting Discount.

**4.3 Delivery and Payment for Option Shares.** Delivery of and payment for the Option Shares shall be made at the offices of Ashford & Pine LLP, 1231 Avenue of the Americas, 34th Floor, New York, New York 10020 (or remotely via the facilities of The Depository Trust Company) on the date of exercise or on such other date as the Representative shall specify (each, an "Additional Closing Date"), in immediately available funds. The Company shall deliver the Option Shares through the facilities of The Depository Trust Company for the account of the Representative against payment by the Representative of the aggregate purchase price therefor.

\newpage

## ARTICLE V — DELIVERY AND PAYMENT

**5.1 Closing.** Delivery of and payment for the Firm Shares shall be made at the offices of Ashford & Pine LLP, 1231 Avenue of the Americas, 34th Floor, New York, New York 10020 (or remotely via the facilities of The Depository Trust Company) at 10:00 a.m., New York time, on March 24, 2025 (the "Closing Date"), which is the third business day following the Pricing Date (T+3 settlement). Such delivery and payment (and the delivery and payment for the Option Shares on each Additional Closing Date) are referred to herein as the "Closing."

**5.2 Delivery of Shares.** The Company shall deliver the Company Shares and the Selling Stockholders shall deliver the Selling Stockholder Shares, in each case through the facilities of The Depository Trust Company for the account of the Representative, against receipt of the purchase price therefor by or on behalf of the Company and the Selling Stockholders, as applicable. The Selling Stockholder Shares shall be delivered by the attorneys-in-fact and custodians appointed under the Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement, acting through Atlantic Stock Transfer & Trust Company, the Company's transfer agent and registrar (the "Transfer Agent").

**5.3 Payment.** Payment for the Firm Shares shall be made by Federal Funds wire transfer in immediately available funds to an account designated by the Company (for the Company Shares) and to an account designated by each Selling Stockholder (for the Selling Stockholder Shares), against delivery of the Firm Shares.

**5.4 Default by an Underwriter.** If any Underwriter defaults in its obligation to purchase Firm Shares, the Representative may, but shall not be obligated to, arrange for another party or parties to purchase such Firm Shares on the terms set forth in this Agreement. If, after reasonable efforts, the Representative is unable to arrange for the purchase of such Firm Shares, the non-defaulting Underwriters shall be obligated, severally in proportion to their respective commitments set forth in Schedule I, to purchase the Firm Shares that the defaulting Underwriter failed to purchase. If the non-defaulting Underwriters are unable to purchase all of such Firm Shares, the Company and the Selling Stockholders shall be entitled to the remedies set forth in Section 9.2, and this Agreement shall terminate as to the unpurchased Firm Shares.

\newpage

## ARTICLE VI — COVENANTS OF THE COMPANY AND THE SELLING STOCKHOLDERS

The Company covenants with each Underwriter as follows:

**6.1 Filing of Final Prospectus.** The Company will file the Final Prospectus with the Commission pursuant to Rule 424(b)(4) under the Act within the time period required thereunder and will furnish to the Representative copies of the Final Prospectus promptly after filing.

**6.2 Amendments and Supplements.** The Company will promptly advise the Representative, and will promptly file an amendment or supplement to the Registration Statement or the Final Prospectus, if (a) any event shall occur as a result of which the Final Prospectus as then amended or supplemented would include an untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact necessary to make the statements therein not misleading, or (b) it is necessary to amend or supplement the Final Prospectus to comply with the Act.

**6.3 Free Writing Prospectuses.** The Company will not file, issue, or distribute any free writing prospectus relating to the Shares without the prior written consent of the Representative, and will promptly advise the Representative of any event that would cause any Issuer Free Writing Prospectus to include an untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact necessary to make the statements therein not misleading.

**6.4 Use of Proceeds.** The Company will apply the net proceeds from the sale of the Company Shares and the Option Shares substantially as described under the caption "Use of Proceeds" in the Final Prospectus, including the repayment of indebtedness under the revolving credit facility in accordance with the mandatory prepayment provisions of the credit agreement governing such facility.

**6.5 Transfer Agent; Listing.** The Company will cooperate with the Transfer Agent and the Underwriters to facilitate the delivery of the Shares at the Closing and any Additional Closing, and will use commercially reasonable efforts to cause the Shares to be listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market prior to the Closing Date.

**6.6 Lock-Up Agreements.** The Company will use commercially reasonable efforts to enforce the provisions of each Lock-Up Agreement and will direct the Transfer Agent to place stop-transfer instructions against the securities subject to such Lock-Up Agreements in accordance with the terms thereof.

**6.7 Reporting.** The Company will, during the period ending on the Closing Date, advise the Representative promptly of any event that would reasonably be expected to result in a Material Adverse Effect or that would cause any representation or warranty of the Company set forth in Article I to be untrue in any material respect.

**6.8 Selling Stockholder Covenants.** Each Selling Stockholder covenants to deliver the Selling Stockholder Shares at the Closing through its attorneys-in-fact and custodians appointed under the Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement and to comply with the terms of its Lock-Up Agreement.

\newpage

## ARTICLE VII — CONDITIONS TO THE OBLIGATIONS OF THE UNDERWRITERS

The obligations of the Underwriters to purchase and pay for the Firm Shares (and the Option Shares) at the Closing (and any Additional Closing) shall be subject to the satisfaction (or waiver by the Representative) of the following conditions:

**7.1 Effectiveness of Registration Statement.** The Registration Statement shall have been declared effective by the Commission under the Act, and no stop order suspending the effectiveness of the Registration Statement shall have been issued, and no proceedings for that purpose shall have been initiated or threatened by the Commission.

**7.2 Filing of Final Prospectus.** The Final Prospectus shall have been filed with the Commission pursuant to Rule 424(b)(4) under the Act within the time period required thereunder.

**7.3 Comfort Letter.** The Underwriters shall have received from Whitman Reese & Co., the Company's independent registered public accounting firm, (a) a comfort letter dated March 18, 2025, addressed to the Representative and the Underwriters, in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Representative, and (b) a bring-down comfort letter dated the Closing Date, in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Representative, confirming the matters covered in the comfort letter and updating the negative assurance through a date no more than three business days prior to the Closing Date.

**7.4 Legal Opinions.** The Underwriters shall have received (a) a legal opinion and negative assurance letter, dated the Closing Date, from Stonebridge & Calloway LLP, counsel to the Company, in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Representative, and (b) a legal opinion, dated the Closing Date, from Ashford & Pine LLP, counsel to the Underwriters, in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Representative.

**7.5 Officers' Certificate.** The Underwriters shall have received a certificate, dated the Closing Date, signed by Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy, Chief Executive Officer of the Company, and David Nishimura, Chief Financial Officer of the Company, to the effect that the representations and warranties of the Company are true and correct in all material respects as of the Closing Date, and that the Company has complied with all agreements and satisfied all conditions on its part to be performed or satisfied prior to the Closing.

**7.6 Secretary's Certificate.** The Underwriters shall have received a certificate, dated the Closing Date, signed by Samantha Reeves, General Counsel and Secretary of the Company, certifying as to (a) the amended and restated certificate of incorporation and amended and restated bylaws of the Company, (b) resolutions of the Board of Directors authorizing the offering and this Agreement, and (c) the incumbency of the officers executing this Agreement and the certificates delivered hereunder.

**7.7 Lock-Up Agreements.** The Underwriters shall have received executed Lock-Up Agreements from all officers, directors, and holders of 1% or more of the outstanding shares of Common Stock (prior to the offering), in form and substance satisfactory to the Representative, including the Springing Extension Annex executed by Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy.

**7.8 Nasdaq Listing.** The Shares shall have been approved for listing on the Nasdaq Global Select Market, subject only to official notice of issuance.

**7.9 No Material Adverse Change.** Since the date of the most recent financial statements included in the Registration Statement, there shall not have occurred any Material Adverse Effect.

**7.10 FINRA Clearance.** The Underwriters shall have received confirmation that the underwriting compensation arrangements have been cleared or are not subject to objection by the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority, Inc. ("FINRA"), and the offering shall comply in all material respects with FINRA Rule 5110 (underwriting compensation not to exceed 8% of gross offering proceeds) and FINRA Rule 5121 (no FINRA-disqualifying conflict of interest).

**7.11 Power of Attorney and Custody Agreement.** The Underwriters shall have received duly executed Power of Attorney and Custody Agreements from each Selling Stockholder, appointing David Nishimura and Samantha Reeves as attorneys-in-fact and custodians.

**7.12 Selling Stockholder Questionnaires.** The Underwriters shall have received completed selling stockholder questionnaires from each Selling Stockholder, in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Representative and Underwriters' Counsel.

**7.13 Additional Documents.** The Underwriters shall have received all such further documents, certificates, and opinions as the Representative may reasonably request.

\newpage

## ARTICLE VIII — INDEMNIFICATION AND CONTRIBUTION

**8.1 Indemnification by the Company.** The Company shall indemnify and hold harmless each Underwriter, its affiliates, directors, officers, employees, and agents, and each person, if any, who controls such Underwriter within the meaning of Section 15 of the Act or Section 20 of the Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), against any and all losses, claims, damages, and liabilities (or actions in respect thereof), joint or several, to which such indemnified party may become subject, arising out of or based upon (a) any untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of a material fact contained in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Pricing Disclosure Package, the Final Prospectus, any Issuer Free Writing Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto, or (b) any omission or alleged omission to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading; provided, however, that the Company shall not be liable to the extent that any such loss, claim, damage, or liability arises out of or is based upon any untrue statement or omission made in reliance upon and in conformity with written information furnished to the Company by or on behalf of any Underwriter specifically for use in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Pricing Disclosure Package, or the Final Prospectus.

**8.2 Indemnification by the Selling Stockholders.** Each Selling Stockholder shall, severally and not jointly, indemnify and hold harmless each Underwriter, its affiliates, directors, officers, employees, and agents, and each person, if any, who controls such Underwriter within the meaning of Section 15 of the Act or Section 20 of the Exchange Act, against any and all losses, claims, damages, and liabilities to which such indemnified party may become subject, arising out of or based upon any untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of a material fact contained in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Pricing Disclosure Package, the Final Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto, or any omission or alleged omission to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading, but only with respect to information furnished in writing by such Selling Stockholder specifically for inclusion in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Pricing Disclosure Package, or the Final Prospectus. Each Selling Stockholder's liability under this Section 8.2 shall not exceed the net proceeds received by such Selling Stockholder from the sale of the Selling Stockholder Shares in the offering.

**8.3 Indemnification by the Underwriters.** Each Underwriter shall, severally and not jointly, indemnify and hold harmless the Company, its directors, each officer of the Company who signed the Registration Statement, each person, if any, who controls the Company within the meaning of Section 15 of the Act or Section 20 of the Exchange Act, and each Selling Stockholder, against any and all losses, claims, damages, and liabilities to which such indemnified party may become subject, arising out of or based upon any untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of a material fact contained in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Pricing Disclosure Package, the Final Prospectus, or any amendment or supplement thereto, or any omission or alleged omission to state a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading, but only to the extent that such untrue statement or omission was made in reliance upon and in conformity with written information furnished to the Company by or on behalf of such Underwriter specifically for use in the Registration Statement, the Preliminary Prospectus, the Pricing Disclosure Package, or the Final Prospectus.

**8.4 Contribution.** If the indemnification provided for in Sections 8.1, 8.2, or 8.3 is unavailable or insufficient to hold harmless an indemnified party, then each indemnifying party shall contribute to the aggregate amount of losses, claims, damages, and liabilities to which such indemnified party may be subject (i) in such proportion as is appropriate to reflect the relative benefits received by the Company, the Selling Stockholders, and the Underwriters from the offering, or (ii) if such allocation is not permitted by applicable law, in such proportion as is appropriate to reflect not only such relative benefits but also the relative fault of the Company, the Selling Stockholders, and the Underwriters. The aggregate contribution of the Underwriters shall not exceed the total Underwriting Discount actually received by the Underwriters. No Selling Stockholder shall be required to contribute an amount in excess of the net proceeds received by such Selling Stockholder from the sale of the Selling Stockholder Shares in the offering. No person found guilty of fraudulent misrepresentation within the meaning of Section 11(f) of the Act shall be entitled to contribution from any person who was not guilty of such fraudulent misrepresentation.

**8.5 Survival.** The indemnification and contribution provisions set forth in this Article VIII shall remain operative and in full force and effect regardless of any investigation made by or on behalf of any Underwriter, the Company, or any Selling Stockholder, and shall survive the delivery of and payment for the Shares and any termination of this Agreement.

\newpage

## ARTICLE IX — TERMINATION

**9.1 Termination by the Representative.** The Representative may terminate this Agreement at any time prior to the Closing Date by notice to the Company and the Selling Stockholders upon the occurrence of any of the following:

(a) any Material Adverse Effect that, in the reasonable judgment of the Representative, makes it impracticable or inadvisable to proceed with the offering;

(b) any suspension or material limitation of trading in securities generally on the Nasdaq Stock Market, the New York Stock Exchange, or any other national securities exchange;

(c) any general moratorium on commercial banking activities declared by federal or New York state authorities;

(d) any material disruption in securities settlement, payment, or clearance services in the United States;

(e) any outbreak or escalation of hostilities, declaration of war, national emergency, act of terrorism, or other calamity or crisis that, in the reasonable judgment of the Representative, materially and adversely affects United States financial markets; or

(f) any downgrade, or public notice of any intended or potential downgrade, of the rating of any securities of the Company or any of its subsidiaries by any nationally recognized statistical rating organization.

**9.2 Effect of Termination.** Upon any termination of this Agreement pursuant to Section 9.1, no party shall have any liability to any other party, except that (a) the Company shall be responsible for the expenses payable by it in accordance with Section 10.6, and (b) the indemnification and contribution provisions set forth in Article VIII shall survive any such termination. If this Agreement is terminated because of the default of an Underwriter, the Company and the Selling Stockholders shall have the right to recover damages from such defaulting Underwriter.

**9.3 Termination by the Company.** The Company may terminate this Agreement prior to the Closing Date if the Underwriters shall have breached or failed to perform any of their obligations hereunder in a manner that would make the Closing impracticable.

\newpage

## ARTICLE X — MISCELLANEOUS

**10.1 Governing Law.** This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to the conflict of laws principles thereof that would require the application of the laws of any other jurisdiction.

**10.2 Jurisdiction; Waiver of Jury Trial.** Any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in the Borough of Manhattan, City of New York, State of New York. Each party hereby irrevocably submits to the personal jurisdiction of such courts and waives any objection to venue therein. EACH PARTY HEREBY IRREVOCABLY WAIVES, TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, ANY AND ALL RIGHTS TO A TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY ACTION, SUIT, OR PROCEEDING ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT.

**10.3 Notices.** All notices, requests, demands, and other communications under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed duly given when delivered by hand, sent by nationally recognized overnight courier, or transmitted by electronic mail with confirmation of receipt, to the parties at the following addresses (or such other address as a party may designate in writing):

*To the Company:*
Meridian Pulse Technologies, Inc., 4200 Clearwater Blvd., Suite 800, Austin, TX 78759, Attention: Samantha Reeves, General Counsel & Secretary; with a copy to Stonebridge & Calloway LLP, 600 Congress Avenue, Suite 2400, Austin, TX 78701, Attention: Jonathan Calloway.

*To the Representative:*
Hargrove Securities LLC, 405 Park Avenue South, 22nd Floor, New York, NY 10016, Attention: Cameron Whitfield, Managing Director, Equity Capital Markets; with a copy to Ashford & Pine LLP, 1231 Avenue of the Americas, 34th Floor, New York, NY 10020, Attention: Victoria Ashford.

*To the Co-Manager:*
Bellweather Capital Markets, Inc., 150 Federal Street, Suite 3100, Boston, MA 02110, Attention: Patrick Donnelly, Managing Director.

*To the Selling Stockholders:*
As set forth on Schedule II hereto.

**10.4 Entire Agreement.** This Agreement, together with the Schedules and Exhibits hereto, constitutes the entire agreement among the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings, and negotiations, whether written or oral, with respect thereto, including the engagement letter dated November 8, 2024, to the extent inconsistent with this Agreement.

**10.5 Amendments and Waivers.** This Agreement may not be amended, modified, or supplemented except by a written instrument signed by the Company, the Representative (on behalf of the several Underwriters), and each Selling Stockholder. No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by the party against whom the waiver is to be enforced. The Representative may waive any condition to the obligations of the Underwriters on behalf of the several Underwriters.

**10.6 Expenses.** The Company shall bear all offering expenses, including SEC registration fees, FINRA filing fees, Nasdaq listing fees, printing and engraving expenses, transfer agent fees, blue sky qualification fees, road show expenses, Company counsel fees (Stonebridge & Calloway LLP), and accounting and auditing fees (Whitman Reese & Co.), estimated at approximately \$3,200,000 in the aggregate. The Underwriters shall bear their own counsel fees and out-of-pocket expenses, including the fees and expenses of Underwriters' Counsel (Ashford & Pine LLP), estimated at approximately \$850,000. Each Selling Stockholder shall bear its own legal counsel fees. The Company shall reimburse any transfer taxes attributable to the Selling Stockholder Shares.

**10.7 Assignment.** This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and permitted assigns. No party may assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other parties, except that an Underwriter may assign its rights and obligations hereunder to an affiliate with the prior written consent of the Representative (not to be unreasonably withheld).

**10.8 Severability.** If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, the validity, legality, and enforceability of the remaining provisions shall not be affected, and such provision shall be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid, legal, and enforceable.

**10.9 Counterparts.** This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts (including by means of facsimile or portable document format (.pdf) signature pages), each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument.

**10.10 Headings.** Section and Article headings herein are for convenience of reference only and shall not affect the interpretation of this Agreement.

**10.11 Representatives' Authority.** The Representative shall act on behalf of the several Underwriters in connection with the exercise of the Over-Allotment Option, the waiver of conditions, the giving of notices, and the termination of this Agreement. The Representative's decisions shall be binding on all Underwriters.

\newpage

## SIGNATURES

This springing extension provision applies solely to Dr. Anand Krishnamurthy and shall not apply to any other officer, director, or stockholder subject to the standard lock-up.
